lore.title: Bear Pit
lore.content:
“
You didn't lose money. You spent it learning a valuable lesson: Never bet against The Lash.
—
Lash
The
Bear Pit
is an underground arena of blood sport in New York where spectators can bet on prize fights.
Summary
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Known fighters include
Lash
and
Bebop
, who are rivals. Lash calls himself a "champion" of the Bear Pit.
[
1
]
Lash has lost to Bebop in the Bear Pit once,
[
2
]
while Bebop lost to Lash at least once afterwards.
[
3
]
[
4
]
Wraith
and
Krill
are prominent gamblers in matches, who are both known to often bet money on Bebop.
[
2
]
[
5
]
Once,
Mendoza
told Wraith that Lash was going to "take a dive" in a Bear Pit fight, which Lash proceeded to win, causing Wraith to lose "a lot of money".
[
6
]
Bebop is fighting in the Bear Pit to earn money for
Ms. Shelly
's medicine.
[
7
]
[
8
]
He intends to retire once he completes the
Ritual
and gets his wish.
[
5
]
Celeste
's friend
Blockhead Lenny
once fought in the Bear Pit, but lost to Lash.
[
9
]
Fights in the Bear Pit seem to allow the use of magic.
[
10
]
Trivia
[
edit
|
edit source
]
In
Neon Prime
,
Kali
was a former champion of the pit who came out of retirement, only to lose to Bebop. Kali sustained injuries severe enough to send him into an almost 2-decade hiatus afterwards.
[
11
]
References
[
edit
|
edit source
]
↑
Lash
And
Seven Conversation 3
-
"You're the pitfighter, yes?"
-
"Uh... You mispronounced "champion"."
↑
2.0
2.1
Lash
And
Wraith Conversation 2
-
"So, you're the lady who bets on Bebop."
-
"You're the guy that lost to Bebop."
-
"One time."
↑
Bebop
's in-game
voice line
(Killed Lash):
"Like I told ya, Lash: beatin' me was a fluke!"
↑
Lash
's in-game
voice line
(Killed Bebop):
"Once again, Lash rights a great wrong!"
↑
5.0
5.1
Bebop
And
Mo & Krill Conversation 2
↑
Lash
And
Wraith Conversation 1
-
"Mendoza said you were gonna take a dive in that fight."
-
"Mendoza says a lot of stupid shit."
-
"I lost a lot of money."
↑
Bebop
's background
↑
Bebop
And
Ivy Conversation 2
↑
Celeste
's in-game
voice line
(killed Lash):
"That’s for what ya did to Lenny in the Bear Pit!"
↑
Shopkeeper
's in-game
voice line
(Lash buys T4 item):
"So is this for a pit-fight, or just recreational mayhem?"
↑
Lash
's in-game
voice line
-
"Not gonna dunk on you, Kali. You were a hell of a fighter before you retired. Lived in obscurity, came out of retirement in a desperate bid for relevancy, only to get injured by Bebop. Ouch! And put on a shelf for the better part of two decades. Uhf."

lore.title: Black Château
lore.content:
The Black Château
is a mysterious location in
Deadlock
, frequented by academics.
Summary
[
edit
|
edit source
]
The Black Château's mysteries were revealed to the general public for the first time in a
New York Oracle
article.
[
1
]
Dynamo
and
Kelvin
last saw each other at the Black Château, before Kelvin's disappearance.
[
2
]
Dynamo says "no one is leaving the Black Château" for
Lady Geist
's gala event.
[
3
]
References
[
edit
|
edit source
]
↑
Newscaster
's in-game
voice line
-
"Have you been curious about the mysteries of the Black Château? For the first time in its august history, the Château's doors are being opened to The New York Oracle."
↑
Dynamo and Kelvin conversation
↑
The Binding of Oathkeeper
-
Chapter 2A

lore.title: Blackmore Academy
lore.content:
Blackmore Academy crest
“
I go to Blackmore. You think money impresses me?
—
Graves
Blackmore Academy
is a school for the children of America's wealthiest families.
Overview
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Blackmore Academy
is a high school attended by
Graves
and
Apollo
.
[
1
]
[
2
]
[
3
]
Mina
and
Pocket
are former students.
[
4
]
[
5
]
Blackmore Academy is an upper-class school, exclusively for wealthy or exceptionally talented students.
[
6
]
[
7
]
Krill
descibes Blackmore Academy as "the breeding ground for Manhattan's finest narcissists".
[
8
]
Graves and Apollo are each other's lab partners.
[
9
]
Blackmore Academy students seem to have the day off on the day of the
ritual
.
[
10
]
Blackmore students are implied to frequent Jezebel's, even though they legally can't drink alcohol.
[
11
]
Infernus
regularly has to kick them out of the bar,
[
12
]
which once happened to Mina on her 16th birthday.
[
13
]
[
14
]
Some Blackmore students play cards at
Wraith
's.
[
15
]
Students
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Apollo
- Student
Graves
- Student
Mina
- Former student (graduated)
Pocket
- Former student
Trivia
[
edit
|
edit source
]
References
[
edit
|
edit source
]
↑
Graves
' background:
"Now a fledgling necromancer, Darcy balances not only her homework at Blackmore Academy... but the demands of graveyard denizens."
↑
Graves
' in-game
voice line
(killed Holliday):
"Shouldn't you be tracking down a serial killer or something, not worrying about a high school kid?"
↑
Apollo
's in-game
voice line
(killed Lash) -
"You couldn't even beat a high school kid, Lash… how sad."
↑
Graves
' in-game
voice line
(killed Mina):
"I hate Blackmore alumni..."
↑
Apollo
And
Pocket Conversation 2
-
"You went to Blackmore? (...) Sounds like you miss it."
↑
Graves
' in-game
voice line
(kill high net worth):
"I go to Blackmore. You think money impresses me?"
↑
Apollo
And
Rem Conversation 3
-
"How can I get a jacket like yours?"
-
"Do you have rich parents? (...) Are you the best at anything?"
↑
Krill
's in-game
voice line
(killed Apollo):
"Blackmore Academy: the breeding ground for Manhattan's finest narcissists."
↑
Graves
' in-game
voice line
(killed Apollo):
"Great, I killed my lab partner..."
↑
Graves
And
Vyper Conversation 1
-
"I have the day off. Shouldn't you be in jail?"
↑
Infernus
' in-game
voice line
(killed Graves):
"When are these Blackmore kids gonna learn?!"
↑
Apollo
And
Infernus Conversation 1
-
"This is gonna shock you, but I'm a grown ass man! I don't hang out with kids from Blackmore Academy: I kick 'em out of my bar."
↑
Mina
's in-game
voice line
(killed Infernus):
"That's for carding me on my 16th birthday."
↑
Graves
And
Infernus Conversation 1
↑
Graves
And
Infernus Conversation 2

lore.title: Brands
lore.content:
Various advertisement billboards in the 66th Store building.
There are many commercial brands advertising their products and services on the streets of the
Cursed Apple
. Here's a list of them and their possible real world inspirations.
Summary
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Afterlife Credit House
Astral Plane
Canned Mille-Pattes Dazzle
Baroness Hotel
-
Baroness Hotel
in Seattle, WA (close to Valve's office in Bellevue)
Black Altar Inc. Coffin Polish
Black Cauldron Brand
Blackwell & Weston Detective Bureau
Bradley's Tonics and Salves
Carpenter Brand Sparkling Brut
- French musician
Carpenter Brut
(named after American filmmaker
John Carpenter
)
Classic Blue Paint Service
C. M. Burns & Ashe Cremations
-
Charles Montgomery Burns
from
The Simpsons,
also a pun on "see them burn"
Crowley's Convenience
-
Aleister Crowley
Dr. Rockwell's Dental Offcie
(sic)
- American artist
Norman Rockwell
Filtered Nationals
Five Eyes from Concentrate Meats
and
Crab 5 Eyes
-
Five Guys Burgers and Fries
Fogwell's Bioluminescence
Friedlander and Koppelman Co. Law and Curse
Golden Loops Corn Cereal
Granite City Garage
-
Granite City, IL
Grebb
Happy Panda Coffee
Hellmans Steel and Iron Works
Hex Jazz Records
Holy Chains
H. West Reanimation Professionals
- H.P. Lovecraft's short story
Herbert West-Reanimator
Liberty Beef Franks
Life Beer
Macksound and Hoff Importers of Occultic Goods
McMahon and Co. Purveyor of Fine Products and Altercations
-
Mann Co.
from
Team Fortress 2
("We sell products and get in fights")
Mosquito Prince
Overlord & Company
Plaza Cigarettes
Prince-S All Brand Guild Society Revue
Red Cross Cola
-
Coca Cola
Red Hand Premium Coffee
- Nick Cave and the Bad Seeds' song
Red Right Hand
Rn Castevets Twin Omen Cigar
-
The Castevets family
from the movie
Rosemary's Baby
Sanctuary Horn Wax
Sanguine Laceration
Talisman Brand Amulets
The 66th Store
-
The 14th Street Store
The New York Oracle
-
The New York Times
Third Eye Supply
-
Third Eye
(spiritual concept)
W.E. Kurtz & Sons
-
Colonel Kurtz
from the movie
Apocalypse Now
White Crow Bleak Thoughts
- In Russian, "White Crow" is an expression similar to
"Black Sheep"
in English
Willeford Pharmaceuticals
- American detective fiction author
Charles Willeford
Items
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Some of the posters are advertising in-game
items
.
Basic Magazine
Health Nova
(item renamed to
Healing Nova
)
Improved Cooldown
Melee Lifesteal
Metal Skin
Mystic Reach
Rescue Beam
Tesla Bullets
Gallery
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Concept art showing various signs
Various signs and artwork
Various signs
Various signs and newspapers
Various signs
Item posters
The New York Oracle billboard
Unused store signs found in the game files
Neon Prime
posters
Trivia
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Concept artist
Evgeniy Evstrastiy
designed some of the billboards.
[
1
]
Some of the fonts used in poster designs are
Hestina
,
Decotura ICG
and
American Captain
.
Happy Panda Coffee replaced a previous poster that read
"Sad Panda Coffee - Always Bitter"
.
Astral Plane Canned Mille-Pattes Dazzle used to be called
Astral Plane Canned Centipedes
in an earlier version of the billboard.
There was an old poster that read
"Prime Neons sold here"
, a reference to
Neon Prime
.
References
[
edit
|
edit source
]
↑
Evgeniy Evstratiy Interview (1:19:00)
on
YouTube
(in Russian)

lore.title: Fairfax Industries
lore.content:
Fairfax Industries logo
“
Yamato
, you run a street gang. I drive the occult industrial complex. You never had a chance.
—
McGinnis
Fairfax Industries
is an arms manufacturing corporation in
Deadlock
. They produce
weapon
items
.
Background
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Fairfax crates
Fairfax Industries is a company in the occult industrial complex that specialises in spirit weaponry,
[
1
]
headed by
Maximilian Fairfax
.
[
2
]
As Maximilian's eldest heir,
Pocket
would have inherited the company before their assassination attempt. Fairfax Industries is described as an "avarice-fueled viper's nest".
[
3
]
They used to have sovereignty of the 50th state of the
US
, the island of Cadenza, which they are currently trying to regain.
[
4
]
According to
McGinnis
, Fairfax was calm during the "Cadenza situation", as he knew that legislation would eventually catch up to him. He's also proud for having created one of the United States. His employees at the time were "rattled", and McGinnis thought she would lose her job.
[
5
]
They are currently under sanction by the Environmental Protection Agency for improperly disposing of spiritual waste.
[
6
]
Various Fairfax-branded
crates
can be found across the
Cursed Apple
. Some stores have curtains with an "FF" logo.
A statue of Walter Fairfax, presumably an ancestor of Pocket and Maximilian, can be found in the garden behind the
Baroness Hotel
.
Fairfax Industries has a publishing arm called
Fairfax Books
.
Dynamo
carries one of their books in his bag.
Eldritch Tactical Solutions
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Eldritch Tactical Solutions
, or
ETS
, is a Paranormal Military Company and a subsidiary of Fairfax Industries. Its workers are known as "soldiers of fortune".
[
7
]
The
New York Oracle
states that it has been accused of committing war crimes by previous employees.
[
8
]
Pocket
wants to dismantle ETS.
[
9
]
Workers
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Maximilian Fairfax
- Current CEO
McGinnis
- Head of the Military R&D department
Wrecker
- Soldier of Fortune for Eldritch Tactical Solutions
Related persons
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Arin "Pocket" Fairfax
- Eldest heir to the corporation. Presumed dead after a failed assassination attempt 5 years ago
Gallery
[
edit
|
edit source
]
CEO Maximilian Fairfax
Walter Fairfax statue
Fairfax Industries Weapon items catalog
A book published by "Fairfax Books"
Store window with "FF" curtains
Trivia
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Fairfax's motto is
"All's Fair!",
a reference to the idiom "All's fair in love and war".
The Fairfax logo in the shop tab uses an edited version of the
DayPosterBlack
font.
References
[
edit
|
edit source
]
↑
The Binding of Oathkeeper
-
Chapter 4A
↑
Newscaster
's in-game
voice line
-
"This month, Maximilian Fairfax made headlines after donating a small fortune to New York orphanages. When asked about his generous act, the CEO of Fairfax Industries replied: "No one should be without family on the holidays"."
↑
Pocket
's background -
"Arin doesn’t know who is trying to keep them from taking control of their parent’s company... but in the avarice fueled viper’s nest that is Fairfax Industries it could be anyone."
↑
Newscaster
's in-game
voice line
-
"The Supreme Court is poised to rule on the Fairfax Fair Tax bill next session. Will our 50th state remain in the union? Or will Fairfax Industries successfully regain its sovereignty over the island of Cadenza? Only time will tell!"
↑
McGinnis and Haze conversation
- McGinnis:
"Honestly, the rest of us were more rattled than he was. I was just a kid freaking out 'cause I thought I was going to lose my job but, Fairfax was surprisingly calm. I don't think he expected Cadenza to last forever. He just wanted to make the most out of an opportunity before legislation caught up. Plus, the guy gets to say he created one of the United States!"
↑
Newscaster
's in-game
voice line
-
"The EPA has issued another sanction to Fairfax Industries. Citing their improper disposal of spiritual waste was not just an affront to nature, but a threat to it!"
↑
Wrecker
's background -
"Employed by Eldritch Tactical Solutions (a subsidiary of Fairfax Industries), Wrecker is an elite soldier of fortune with a reputation for doing dirty work that other PMCs don’t have the stomach for."
↑
Newscaster
's in-game
voice line
-
"Eldritch Tactical Solutions, a subsidiary of Fairfax Industries, comes under fire as new allegations are made by former employees. Are these soldiers of fortune committing war crimes? The New York Oracle investigates."
↑
Pocket
's in-game
voice line
-
"I'll dismantle ETS if it's the last thing I do."

lore.title: Friends of Humanity
lore.content:
“
There's a Friends of Humanity safehouse I need to burn down.
—
Grey Talon
The
Friends of Humanity
are a "neo inquisition" political group founded by the descendants of
John Hathorne
.
[
1
]
[
2
]
Their leader is also named
John Hathorne
, described as a "political firebrand"
[
3
]
and a "controversial speaker".
[
4
]
Summary
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Vindicta
wants to kill John Hathorne and eradicate the Friends of Humanity.
[
5
]
[
6
]
[
7
]
She has already shot John's brother.
[
8
]
Grey Talon
suspects the group's members were behind the murder of his family, and is similarly pursuing them.
[
9
]
[
10
]
Hathorne has survived an assassination attempt.
[
4
]
if Vindicta doesn't complete the ritual, she plans to assassinate him during his next public appearance at Columbia University.
[
11
]
Haze
warns Vindicta that murdering Hathorne will "galvanize" the Friends of Humanity.
[
12
]
Mo & Krill
don't like the Friends of Humanity and support Grey Talon's actions.
[
13
]
Infernus
similarly supports Vindicta.
[
14
]
The Friends of Humanity have rallied in New York to protest against the
US Government
's aid to
Ixia
, amid the Ixian War.
[
2
]
References
[
edit
|
edit source
]
↑
Vindicta
's background -
"Waging a one ghost war against the "The Friends of Humanity", a neo inquisition formed by Hathorne's descendants; Vindicta looks to keep the supernatural safe from Hathorne’s ilk"
↑
2.0
2.1
Newscaster
's in-game
voice line
-
"As the military campaign in Ixia continues, the controversial political organization Friends of Humanity announced a rally in New York to demand the US cease sending aid to the Outer Planes."
↑
Newscaster
's in-game
voice line
-
"Political firebrand? Or founder of a new inquisition? Read our exclusive interview with John Hathorne and decide for yourself!"
↑
4.0
4.1
Newscaster
's in-game
voice line
-
"An attempt on John Hathorne's life has raised security concerns for the controversial speaker's upcoming New York appearance."
↑
Vindicta
's in-game
voice line
-
"I don't need the Patrons to kill Hathorne."
↑
Vindicta
's in-game
voice line
-
"The Friends of Humanity will be eradicated from this Earth."
↑
Amber Hand
's in-game
voice line
-
"Complete the ritual, Vindicta, and Hathorne's bloodline will be eradicated from this planet."
↑
Vindicta and Holliday conversation
- Vindicta:
"I shot Hathorme's brother while he was day-drinking on a speedboat."
↑
Grey Talon
's in-game
voice line
-
"Can't. There's a Friends of Humanity safehouse I need to burn down."
↑
Sapphire Flame
's in-game
voice line
-
"Hathorne's disciples will answer for their crimes. Together, we will make them suffer in ways unimaginable!"
↑
Vindicta and Dynamo conversation
↑
Haze and Vindicta conversation
- Haze:
"Your war with the Friends of Humanity. Murdering John Hathorne will galvanize them."
↑
Mo & Krill and Grey Talon conversation
- Krill:
"You're the man making life hell for the Friends of Humanity, yes?"
Grey Talon:
"I am."
Krill:
"Thank you."
↑
Infernus
And
Vindicta Conversation 3
-
"Heard you're the one taking out the Friends of Humanity. (...) I ain't losing sleep."

lore.title: Heroes
lore.content:
Heroes
Attributes Table
Release Date
Heroes
are the playable characters of
Deadlock
.
There are currently
38
heroes available, with more to be released as the game develops.
Overview
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Heroes come in a wide variety in Deadlock, each with their own specialisations and playstyles. Each hero wields their own unique primary weapon which has its own name, stats and weapon type. Weapons are affected by
Weapon
Class
Items
and are the primary means of dealing damage for most heroes. Some weapons possess an alternate fire mode activated with
RMB
.
Besides their weapons, heroes also possess 4 unique abilities with a variety of effects. Some abilities are completely passive, and will always be active regardless (such as
Abrams
'
Infernal Resilience
), though most are simply activated with their respective bindings (
1
,
2
,
3
,
4
by default). Abilities come with up to 3 upgrades, which can be purchased with an increasing number of
Ability Points
. Abilities most often scale with
Spirit Power
.
Heroes have set base stats that they can improve through
leveling up
or purchasing
items
. They also have additional
Power Increases
, gained at specific levels which grant bonuses to specific stats. Heroes can hold up to 12 individual items at a time, so long as they conform to their categories and
Extra Slots
. Some hero stats are also affected by
Spirit Power
, referred to as Spirit Scaling.
38
heroes are available in the current alpha build, with additional characters confirmed for future releases.
Heroes
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Hero Labs
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Hero Labs
was an alternative game mode where players could test unfinished heroes.
The following Heroes were only accessible in Hero Labs, before the mode was discontinued.
Unused Heroes
[
edit
|
edit source
]
There are a number of heroes in
Deadlock
that can only be accessed using
console commands
, or that were removed from the game's code. Some of them are older heroes inherited from
Neon Prime
, while others were added to the game more recently.
Boho
The Boss
Cadence
Druid
Fortuna
Graf
Gunslinger
Kali
Rutger
Skyrunner
Swan
Thumper
Tokamak
Vandal
Trivia
[
edit
|
edit source
]
At one point in early development, the Heroes were called
Fates
.
[
1
]
The Hero backstories were previously called
"A Fate's Tale"
.
In the French localization, the Heroes are called
Occultistes
.
References
[
edit
|
edit source
]
↑
2024-08-14
Yoshi
; Deadlock Discord -
"We had them called The Fates at some point earlier on, but we stuck with heroes for now. We'll probably rename them in the future."
{{#seo:
|description=Deadlock's 38 Heroes come in a wide variety, each with their own specialisations and playstyles. Each hero wields their own unique primary weapon which has its own name, stats and weapon type. Weapons are affected by
Weapon
Class
Items
and are the primary means of dealing damage for most heroes. Besides their weapons, heroes wield strange and spiritual abilities that may inflict damage, apply buffs, or otherwise affect the outcome of a match.
|keywords=Deadlock, Characters, Deadlock characters, Heroes, Hero, Roster, MOBA, New heroes, Shooter, Game, Valve
|image=DeadlockWikiSEO.png}}

lore.title: Hideout
lore.content:
The main area of the Hideout.
The Hideout
is a personal, interactive hub that functions as the main menu and social area in
Deadlock
. Within this space, players can freely move around, prepare for matches, and invite friends to join them. Various terminals located throughout the Hideout provide access to the game's core menus, including Play, Watch, and Learn.
For training, the Hideout includes a dedicated practice range called The Atrium, designed for testing hero movement and abilities without a loading screen. Players can access the more extensive Sandbox mode through a
Teleporter
found within this practice area.
Overview
[
edit
|
edit source
]
The Hideout serves as an immersive replacement for traditional 2D menus, with players loading into this space upon launching the game. The environment is a grand, multi-level manor room with an opulent, gothic atmosphere, featuring a large fireplace, an extensive library, and towering windows that offer a view of
The Cursed Apple
at dusk. This polished main hall stands in contrast to the adjoining Atrium, which has a more raw, industrial design.
Layout
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Players spawn in the center of the main hall.
Directly in front is the
Play Terminal
, which serves as the room's focal point.
To the right is the
Learn to Play Terminal
, situated in a cozy library nook by a fireplace.
To the left, a path leads towards the
Shop & Atrium
.
In the back-right corner of the room is a separate viewing lounge that houses the
Watch Terminal
.
A large, ornate staircase dominates the back of the room, leading to an upper balcony. The left side of the balcony is a furnished trophy room, while the right side is an undeveloped storage area filled with crates and sandbags.
Functional Areas
[
edit
|
edit source
]
The main functionalities of the game are accessed through distinct stations scattered around the room. Interacting with a terminal opens a full-screen UI menu.
Play Terminal
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Located in the center of the room, the Play Terminal is the primary station for matchmaking. It appears as a circular table surrounded by a glowing green summoning circle. Interacting with it opens the "Select Play Mode" menu, which includes options for:
Deadlock:
Standard player-vs-player matches.
Street Brawl
:
Quick-paced 4v4 battle.
Bot Match:
Play against AI bots, either privately or with other players.
Sandbox Mode:
A comprehensive training mode with advanced tools.
Explore NYC:
A mode to freely explore the map.
Custom Match:
Create private lobbies with custom rules.
Learn to Play Terminal
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Situated in the library area to the right of the main hall, this terminal is a book resting on a stand in front of an armchair. Interacting with it provides access to the game's Basic Tutorial. This is also accessible from the Play Terminal for new players.
Watch Terminal
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Found in a back room, the Watch Terminal consists of a vintage film projector aimed at a screen. This station is used to access the replay and spectator system, allowing players to watch live matches or search for a specific match by its ID.
News Terminal
[
edit
|
edit source
]
The news terminal is a corner under the stairs with newspapers, magazines and a mystic personal computer powered by candles and using an Ouija planchette as a mouse. Interacting with the terminal shows news about recent updates published on Steam.
Vote Terminal
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Found on a table near the entrance to the Shop & Practice Space, the Vote Terminal is a wooden ballot box that allowed the community to influence which Hero to be added next. Interacting with it opened the "Who's Next?" menu, where players were able to cast votes for which hero they would like to see released next. Players earned one vote for each match they play. A timer on the UI indicated when the next hero unlock was scheduled to occur. Voting added stickers to the walls:
Six New Heroes
:
"Eye Voted" stickers
Old Gods, New Blood
:
Stickers depicting the face of the hero voted for
Shop & Atrium
[
edit
|
edit source
]
The antechamber containing The Curiosity Shop.
To the left of the main hall is an open doorway leading to a two-part training area.
The Curiosity Shop
[
edit
|
edit source
]
The antechamber to the practice range features a fully-stocked counter for
The Curiosity Shop
. While players can access the item shop at any time with a dedicated keybind.
The Atrium
[
edit
|
edit source
]
The main practice area is a large, industrial-looking room with scaffolding and graffiti. It is designed for basic ability and damage testing.
Bots:
The range contains one friendly and one enemy bot. These bots are static and do not perform any actions, serving as simple targets.
Training Tools:
A large, red-buttoned machine allows players to toggle fast cooldowns and infinite stamina by meleeing it.
When in a party, a crystal appears that allows players to swap teams.
Teleporter to Sandbox:
At the back of the range is a
teleporter
that allows players to instantly load into the full Sandbox mode for more advanced training options.
Animals:
The Atrium has a cat and a flying owl. The cat chases the ball, as well as
Calico
in
Ava
's form, the laser pointer from
Vindicta
's
rifle
,
The Doorman
's
Call Bell
, and
Viscous
in
Goo Ball
form.
Kicking the ball into the teleporter 3 times will spawn a rabbit. The rabbit has the same behavior as the cat. If the cat and the rabbit come into proximity of each other, and aren't already chasing one of the aforementioned objects, the cat will chase the rabbit around the room.
Shooting the owl makes it disappear and spawns a new owl, as well as a second one, increasing the number of owls by one every time all owls are shot. It also shows a counter of how many owls were shot by the player in the current session.
Customization
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Winter 2025 Souvenir
The Hideout is planned to be customizable with items unlocked by the player. The first two unlockables are:
A snow globe above the fireplace for players who engaged in the
Winter 2024
event challenges.
A Merry Midtown Mayhem Souvenir for players who participated in the
Winter 2025
event.
Status
[
edit
|
edit source
]
The Hideout has a unique
Rich Presence
status for each hero that appears for friends and players in a party in the main menu, as well as on the Steam friends menu.
Gallery
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Hideout window view (title screen)
Trivia
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Shadowy silhouettes of
Billy
,
The Doorman
,
Drifter
,
Mina
,
Paige
, and
Victor
could be seen throughout the Hideout before their release during the
Six New Heroes
update. Similarly, shadowy silhouettes of
Apollo
,
Celeste
,
Graves
,
Rem
,
Silver
and
Venator
could be seen throughout the Hideout before their release during the
Old Gods, New Blood
update.
On
20th Janurary 2026
, the
Old Gods, New Blood
update's heroes were teased with various objects scattered around the Hideout (such as
Apollo
's sword,
Rem
's pillow and
Venator
's thurible).
The clock, located above the elevator in the hideout, displays the player's local system time while playing solo. However, upon joining a party, it switches to reflect the local time in Seattle, Washington (PST/PDT), the location of Valve's headquarters. The clock also flashes and dings every 60 seconds. On the hour, it also chimes to match the current hour.
See Also
[
edit
|
edit source
]
The Cursed Apple

lore.title: Holliday's visual novel
lore.content:
“This is going to be exciting!”
This page contains game content that is unfinished, work in progress, or unavailable in standard gameplay.
Holliday's visual novel
is an upcoming
Deadlock
visual novel
.
The novel is accessible in-game through the
console command
citadel_book_open 4
, but only the audio is available and it is currently incomplete.
See also
Holliday's visual novel/Transcription
.
Characters
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Holliday
Captain Murphy
Agent Marlowe
Allie Kinkaid
Chapters
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Chapter 1
[
edit
|
edit source
]
1 - Welcome to New York
Holliday arrives in
New York
in pursuit of a serial killer.
Chapter 2
[
edit
|
edit source
]
2a - Jurisdiction
Holliday reunites with an old colleague.
2b - Deep Conversations At The Waffle House
Holliday gets a tip from her reporter friend.
Chapter 3
[
edit
|
edit source
]
3a - Dreamwalk
Holliday agrees to help the
OSIC
.
3d - Fateful Meeting
Holliday meets Allie's source.
Chapter 4
[
edit
|
edit source
]
4a - The February House
Holliday closes in on the Troubadour.
Chapter 5
[
edit
|
edit source
]
5b - One Last Song
Holliday makes her choice.
Trivia
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Holliday's post-visual novel
conversations
give context to where some of the story paths will go. Based on these lines,
Haze
will help Holliday in the OSIC path and
Seven
will help her in the Allie Kinkaid path.
Mo & Krill
are also expected to appear in Holliday's visual novel based on an unfinished conversation between them and Holliday, although it's unknown what role they will play in the story.
Holliday's friend and Allie Kinkaid share their voice actors with
Trapper
and
Savannah (Sinclair)
, respectively.

lore.title: Ixia
lore.content:
Apollo
, the "Prince of Ixia"
“
His son's wife was an Ixian who could snuff out flame with a thought.
—
Grey Talon's
background
Ixia
is an
Outer Plane
connected to Earth through an
astral gate
.
[
1
]
Summary
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Ixia is described as a beautiful place and a popular tourism destination.
[
2
]
[
3
]
[
4
]
The US government is sending aid to Ixia as part of their military campaign, drawing opposition from the
Friends of Humanity
.
[
5
]
The Ixian War is a divisive political topic.
[
6
]
Apollo
is referred to as
The Prince of Ixia
[
7
]
or
Crown Prince of North Ixia,
though he finds the title inaccurate.
[
8
]
His father is referred to as a warlord, and has the authority equivalent to a governor and a general.
[
9
]
Apollo wants to represent Ixia in the Olympics.
[
10
]
Wrecker
is known as
The Butcher of Ixia.
[
11
]
He fought in the fields of North Ixia.
[
12
]
Kelvin
has done an expedition to Ixia.
[
13
]
Grey Talon
has visited South Ixia.
[
3
]
South Ixia
[
edit
|
edit source
]
South Ixia
is the 52nd state of the
United States
. The anniversary of its annexation is celebrated across the country.
[
14
]
North Ixia
[
edit
|
edit source
]
North Ixia
is a sovereign region described as "a land of beauty, tradition and conflict", in which notable battles have occurred. It is plagued by political violence, which forced Apollo to move to
New York
.
[
2
]
[
12
]
[
15
]
Ixians
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Ixians
are beings native to Ixia, colloquially called
demons
by some, a term that
Apollo
finds offensive.
[
16
]
[
17
]
[
18
]
After the first
Maelstrom
, some Ixians have immigrated to Earth, leading to a new population of Earth-born Ixians.
Infernus
and the daughter-in-law of
Grey Talon
are some of them.
[
1
]
[
19
]
[
20
]
Ixians are characterized by a demon-like appearance with horns and a tail (with the exception of Infernus) and a variety of skin colors including red, blue and black. Some of them have a supernatural control of fire, manifested in Infernus and in Grey Talon's daughter-in-law.
Infernus has never visited Ixia, as he was born and raised in
New York
.
Abrams
is surprised to learn this, implying that he has visited Ixia.
[
21
]
Infernus doesn't speak Ixian nor is familiar with the language, as his parents only speak English around him. He believes they moved to New York because they were unhappy in Ixia.
[
19
]
Apollo is surprised that Infernus does not recognize him.
[
22
]
Cadence
is a popular Ixian singer.
[
23
]
It seems to be possible for Ixians to have children with humans.
[
20
]
It's currently unknown if these "half-Ixians" inherit all the Ixian traits from their parent.
Apollo uses "gods" as an interjection, indicating that Ixians may have a
politheistic
religion.
[
24
]
[
25
]
[
26
]
Known Ixians
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Abrams
[
27
]
Apollo
("Prince of Ixia") and his father ("Warlord of Ixia")
Cadence
Infernus
and his family
Grey Talon
's daughter-in-law (deceased)
Language
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Ixia has its own language. As a native Ixian,
Apollo
is fluent.
Rem
and
Seven
use Ixian phrases.
[
28
]
[
29
]
Infernus
doesn't speak Ixian.
[
19
]
Examples of Ixian sentences:
"Itani Lo Sahn"
(Apollo's ultimate) -
"I have no equal"
[
30
]
"Hasha Vogali" - "Death's Whisper"
[
31
]
"Shioth valosh, Apollo."
(Either a farewell or an insult)
"Sirosi galah, Infernus."
(Presumably a greeting)
Gallery
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Abrams
Apollo
Infernus
Arch apartment Ixia
building type
Ixia hair braiding and Conjuring
sign (unused)
Trivia
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Infernus
has a tail in
The Binding of Oathkeeper
artwork and in one of his earlier models.
Abrams
has spikes in
The Binding of Oathkeeper
artwork.
Ixia's name may be a reference to the nine circles of Hell in
Dante's Inferno
, as IX is the Roman numeral for 9.
It also shares its name with the planet
Ix
from
Dune
, whose inhabitants are also known as Ixians.
One of the building types in the
Cursed Apple
is labeled
"Arch_apartment_ixia"
in the game files.
There is an unused texture for a sign advertising
"Ixia hair braiding and Conjuring"
.
In a removed voice line,
Dynamo
describes a fellow professor's birth place as an "infernal hellscape", presumably a reference to Ixia.
[
32
]
Grey Talon
's background originally read
"his son's wife was Ixian... she could snuff out flame with a thought".
The new wording
("an Ixian who could snuff out flames")
removes the implication that all Ixians can control fire.
References
[
edit
|
edit source
]
↑
1.0
1.1
2024-09-03 -
Yoshi
;
Deadlock Discord
-
"Ixia is one of the outer planes that is connected to our world via an astral gate. That is the plane that characters that we refer to as 'demons' would have historical roots in. That being said though, at this time in the world of Deadlock there are plenty of Ixians that are born on Earth: Infernus and Grey Talon's daughter-in-law (RIP). There are military operations happening in Ixia right now."
↑
2.0
2.1
Apollo
's background -
"A land of beauty, tradition and conflict; North Ixia is no stranger to brutal political violence. So when Apollo was told that it was for his own safety he was being sent to New York for school, he knew the situation was serious."
↑
3.0
3.1
Dynamo and Grey Talon conversation
- Dynamo:
"I hear that South Ixia is beautiful."
Grey Talon:
"It is."
↑

's in-game
voice line
-
"Looking at taking a vacation to Ixia? Read The New York Oracle for helpful travel tips to the Outer Planes."
↑

"It's a cultural thing. You don't have a word in English for someone who wields the power of a governor and a general the way we do in Ixia."
↑
Apollo
's in-game
voice line
-
"Mark my words, I will represent Ixia in the Olympics. It's only a matter of time."
↑
Amber Hand

-

↑

12.1
Amber Hand
's in-game
voice line
-
"This may not be the fields of North Ixia, but it is a battleground, and today you will slaughter them all."
↑
Kelvin and Mo & Krill conversation
- Krill:
"I actually moved quite a few pieces from your Ixian expedition."
↑
Newscaster
's in-game
voice line
-
"Next week will mark the 5 year anniversary of South Ixia officially joining the United States as its 52nd state. See how folks are planning to celebrate across the country in today's edition of The New York Oracle."
↑
Apollo
And
Vindicta Conversation 2
-
"As someone whose life has been affected by political assassinations, I have... complicated feelings."
↑
Apollo
And
Venator Conversation 2
-
"Men like you call people like me demons, so no. I don't trust you."
↑
Venator
's in-game
voice line
(Unkillable enemy Abrams) -
"Die, demon!"
↑
Warden
's in-game
voice line
(killed Abrams) -
"It's over, demon."
↑
19.0
19.1
19.2
Apollo
And
Infernus Conversation 4
-
"English is the only language my parents will speak around me. (...) All I know is that If my parents thought living in Ixia was so great, they never would've left."
↑
20.0
20.1
Grey Talon
's background -
"[Grey Talon] doted on his grandkids. (...) His son's wife was an Ixian who could snuff out flame with a thought."
↑
Infernus and Abrams conversation
- Abrams:
"So when's the last time you went to Ixia?"
Infernus:
"Never."
Abrams:
"You gotta be kidding me!"
Infernus:

↑

-

-

's in-game
voice line

's in-game   (ally Haze Bullet Dance multikill) -

↑

-

's in-game

2026-02-12 -
Yoshi
; Deadlock Discord -
"[It] means 'I have no equal'"
↑

-

↑

voice line
-
"The night he got tenured, Zurkzark said I paved the way for him with the university. it's a flattering thought, but I knew there was no one better to teach theoretical abstract referential geometry than someone who was born in an infernal hellscape."

lore.title: Jezebel's
lore.content:
Jezebel's Billiard Hall from The Binding of Oathkeeper.
“
You should stop by Jezebel's sometime. If you like people watching, you'll see all types there.
—
Infernus
Jezebel's is a sketchy yet popular bar located in New York.
Summary
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Jezebel's is an old church renovated into a billiard's hall and bar. The place is run by a man named
Hank
and tended to by
Infernus
, who doubles as the bar's bouncer.
[
1
]
Jezebel's is described in The New York Oracle as a place where people can bet their souls over a game of pool.
[
2
]
Shortly before the events of the game take place, Jezebel's is under threat of getting shut down by the Alchemy Bureau due to a large number of infractions, including gambling souls without a license, distributing spirits from Ixia without a permit, and not having the Hex & Alchemy Bureau authorize a ward at the bar's entrance. Infernus and Hank are given 2 weeks to resolve the infractions.
[
3
]
Hank
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Hank
Hank is a portly, middle-aged man who owns Jezebel's.
[
4
]
[
5
]
He's worried about the bar's infractions and is willing to "make a deal with the devil himself" in order to save his bar.
[
6
]
[
7
]
The threat of Jezebel's being shut down and what it means for Hank is why Infernus is participating in the ritual.
[
8
]
[
9
]
Since Infernus doesn't want Hank to worry about him, he hasn't told Hank about his involvement with the ritual.
[
10
]
Infernus mentions that Hank "saved his life".
[
11
]
The implication seems to be that Hank helped Infernus move on from his criminal youth and turn over a new leaf as a bartender.
[
12
]
Hank is also implied to be friends with
Celeste
,
[
10
]
Lady Geist
,
[
7
]
and
Mo & Krill
.
[
13
]
In one pathway in
The Binding of Oathkeeper
, Lady Geist pawns off Oathkeeper to Hank on the promise of getting Hank the resources needed to save Jezebel's.
[
7
]
Gallery
[
edit
|
edit source
]
All images are from
The Binding of Oathkeeper
.

Booth on the second floor
Biliard's table

|

[
2
]

describes Jezebel's as "too fancy".
[
14

The characters that currently frequent Jezebel's include but aren't limited to

the

Vyper

lore.title: Johann Geist Jr.
lore.content:
Johann Geist Jr. from The Binding of Oathkeeper
“
At this moment, his two favorite hobbies are medicine, and being sanctimonious.
— Lady Geist
Doctor Johann Geist Jr. is the grandson of
Lady Geist
.
Summary
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Johann Geist Jr. is a doctor born in
New York
.
[
1
]
He is the son of
Walter Geist
, and the grandson of
Johann Geist
and
Lady Jeanne Geist
.
Johann Jr.'s known patients include Lady Geist and
Ms. Shelly
. Like his father before him, Johann Jr. repairs and maintains the ward Lady Geist uses to contain
Oathkeeper
.
Johann is aware that Lady Geist kills people to feed Oathkeeper, although he hasn't seen any of her victims before
The Binding of Oathkeeper
.
[
2
]
Johann shows contempt towards Oathkeeper and has gotten into arguments with Lady Geist in the past over him.
[
3
]
Lady Geist refers to Johann as "one of the best doctors in New York,"
[
4
]
although she also thinks he can be sanctimonious.
[
5
]
Johann holds his father in high regard.
[
6
]
The Binding of Oathkeeper
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Content below contains spoilers for
The Binding of Oathkeeper
Johann Jr. is one of the main characters in Lady Geist's visual novel:
The Binding of Oathkeeper
. His role and fate depends on what choices Lady Geist makes during the story.
In Chapter 1, Lady Geist calls Johann Jr. over to inspect Oathkeeper's weakening ward. Johann tells Geist that Oathkeeper is getting close to breaking the ward and that he can't build a better ward. After getting threatened by Lady Geist, Johann suggests learning more about Oathkeeper, or learning more about Wards.
[
7
]
Johann has little to no impact in storylines A and B past Chapter 1, beyond contacting
Dynamo
to talk to Lady Geist in Chapter 2a.
[
8
]
Storyline C, D, E and F
[
edit
|
edit source
]
In Chapter 2b, Johann Jr. tells Lady Geist what he learned about Oathkeeper from Walter Geist's research.
In Chapter 4d, Lady Geist wants to transfer Oathkeeper to Johann Jr., which he passionately refuses. Johann suggests Geist give Oathkeeper to some other terrible person she knows such as
Maximilian Fairfax
, but takes it back once Geist points out that Fairfax would likely weaponize Oathkeeper for the
Occult Industrial Complex
. After Lady Geist suggests giving Oathkeeper to
Shelly Fisher
, Johann snaps and tells Geist that there's no cure for Oathkeeper and that her time is up before leaving.
Johann doesn't appear again in storyline C, D, E or F, but he does contact Ms. Shelly to warn her about Lady Geist and Oathkeeper.
[
9
]
Storyline G and H
[
edit
|
edit source
]
In Chapter 3c, Lady Geist decides to try and play off Oathkeeper's cruelty. To do this, they plan to summon the Patrons to keep Oathkeeper obedient for 200 years at the cost of Johann Jr.'s life.
The plan is put into action in Chapter 3d, where Lady Geist calls Johann over for a cup of tea. Johann asks about Oathkeeper, where Geist reveals the plan to make a deal with the Patrons. Johann asks why she doesn't just banish Oathkeeper, as he believed the point of the Oathkeeper problem was to get rid of him. Lady Geist reveals that she has no intention of giving up Oathkeeper, which causes Johann to refuse to have any part in Geist's plan. Unfortunately for Johann, the tea he drank was drugged, causing him to fall unconscious.
In Chapter 5g, Lady Geist succeeds in summoning the Patron and takes Johann Jr. to them. She demands a sacrifice: Johann's life for 200 years of obidience from Oathkeeper. Johann begs Geist to stop, while Oathkeeper consents to the deal. Johann is killed as the Patron grants the wish, leaving his bloody body lying in an alleyway.
In Chapter 5h, Lady Geist succeeds in summoning the Patron, just like in Chapter 5g. Right before she makes her wish, she remembers the time she first held Johann Jr. as a baby, with her son Walter Geist by her side. The memories of her family causes Lady Geist to change her wish at the last moment, sacrificing herself to banish Oathkeeper from Earth's realm. Some time passes as Johann visits the Geist family's grave, and while he's still a little shaken by Geist almost killing him, he ultimately thanks her.
Storyline I
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Beyond Chapter 2b, Johann Jr. doesn't appear in Storyline I until Chapter 5, after Lady Geist succeeds in summoning the Patron and seperates herself from Oathkeeper. Deciding to enjoy the rest of her life in Paris, Geist and Johann wait for her flight at an airport. Geist thanks Johann for his help and tells him he's very much his father's son, which he appreciates. She also leaves him a bag of valuables as payment for being a difficult patient for Johann.
Trivia
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Lady Geist
thinks Johann Jr. would like
Paige
.
[
10
]
Johann Jr. occasionally refers to
Lady Geist
as "Oma", which means "Grandma" in German and Dutch.
[
11
]
[
12
]
Johann Jr. and
Lady Geist
have the same eyes.
[
13
]
Infernus
is familar with Johann Jr. He thinks Johann is a "good kid."
[
5
]
References
[
edit
|
edit source
]

lore.title: Lore
lore.content:
Cursed Apple concept art
“
This story isn't over yet.
—
Ivy
Hero backstory icon
Lore
is the story of the world of
Deadlock
and its characters.
The Lore of
Deadlock
is pieced together from various sources: Hero backstories,
voice lines
, the environment, and the
visual novels
.
Hero backstories can be found in-game in the lower right corner button of the hero select screen.
Storyline
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Main article:
Storyline
The supernatural always existed in the world of
Deadlock,
but only when an eclipse triggered an event known as the
Maelstrom
near the end of the 19th century, which opened
Astral Gates
across the Earth, did the world at large accept it wasn't just myth.
Now, 50 years later, two
Patrons
, the
Hidden King
and the
Archmother
, are trying to enter this plane, promising to fulfill the wish of anyone that helps them by completing the ritual. The ritual includes two teams fighting each other in a battle in the streets of the
Cursed Apple
, trying to prevent the other team from summoning their patron.
Groups
[
edit
|
edit source
]
There are various organizations featured in
Deadlock:
Occult Security and Investigation Commission (OSIC)
– A secretive government organization that employs
Haze
.
The Baxter Society
– An organization of monster hunters whose members include
Shiv
, and formerly
Grey Talon
and
Calico
.
The Tunnel Rats
– A staple of New York’s criminal underground that counts
Mo & Krill
as enforcers and smugglers.
The New York Oracle
– Bringing the latest news and occult discoveries to the citizens of New York.
Fairfax Industries
– A powerful company spearheading the occult industrial complex that employs
McGinnis
and is connected to
Pocket
.
The Friends of Humanity
– A group of xenophobic extremists prioritizing humanity’s safety above all else, drawing opposition from
Vindicta
and
Grey Talon
.
The Mendoza Syndicate
– A crime syndicate dismantled by
Ivy
that once ruled the streets of Spanish Harlem.
The Seventh Moon
- A crime family from Japan led by
Yamato
, looking to get power in America.
The Municipal Coven
– The caretakers of New York.
The Paradox Organisation
- A group of time-thieves who attempt high-stake heists for personal challenge and satisfaction.
There are also a variety of
brands
that advertise their products and services in the Cursed Apple.
Locations
[
edit
|
edit source
]
The world of
Deadlock
includes a wide variety of environments:
The Cursed Apple
– The central setting of Deadlock, where the ritual to summon the Patrons takes place.
The Astral Gates
– The primary connections between Earth and other planes of existence.
Ixia
– One of the extraplanar locations beyond the Astral Gates, where demon-like beings like
Apollo
reside.
The Deep
– A peculiar deep-sea habitat where
Viscous
and
Fathom
come from.
The Bear Pit
– An underground fighting arena where
Wraith
and
Mendoza
make bets, often between
Lash
and
Bebop
.
Jezebel’s Billiard Hall
– A renovated church where
Infernus
serves drinks.
The Paradox Museum
– An exhibit where
Paradox
’s stolen relics are publicly showcased.
Lost Whisper
– The oubliette that
Seven
escaped from.
The Baroness Hotel
- The luxurious hotel where
The Doorman
serves.
Blackmore Academy
- The wealthy high school that
Apollo
and
Graves
attend.
Visual novels
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Main article:
The Library
Visual novels are planned to be the main narrative medium to expand the world of Deadlock.
The Binding of Oathkeeper
,
starring
Lady Geist
, shows the ward binding Oathkeeper weakening, forcing Lady Geist to find a way to stop Oathkeeper from breaking free and killing her.
Ivy's visual novel
follows
Ivy
trying to find a new purpose in life after dismantling the Mendoza Syndicate.
Holliday's visual novel
follows
Holliday
on her revenge-fuelled quest to take down the Troubadour.
Infernus
and
Kelvin
are also planned to have visual novels according to notes from
The Binding of Oathkeeper
, with the first chapter of Infernus' novel referenced in the game files, but neither had further development thus far. References to a
Mina
visual novel were also datamined after the
Old Gods, New Blood
update.
External Links
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Character Relationship Chart
{{#seo:
|description=Deadlock's Lore focuses on the ways that the supernatural affected Earth when an eclipse triggered an event known as the Maelstrom near the end of the 19th century. The many characters, locations, and stories of Deadlock revolve around this new supernaturally-involved society, as well as the combat taking place to earn wishes from Patrons.
|keywords=Deadlock, Lore, Deadlock lore, story, characters, NPCs, Valve, Game, MOBA, Shooter
|image=City_concept_art.png}}

lore.title: Lost Whisper
lore.content:
“
Sometimes I think "Man, it would be so fun to break out of Lost Whisper", and then I think "V, that is a dumbass idea".
—
Vyper
Lost Whisper
is an
oubliette
(dungeon prison) for "the most dangerous occultists".
Summary
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Seven
escaped from Lost Whisper following a failed attempt to execute him via obliteration of the soul.
[
1
]
Rumors have spread about his escape.
[
2
]
Lady Geist
doesn't want to spend the rest of her life "in an
OSIC
oubliette".
[
3
]
References
[
edit
|
edit source
]
↑
Seven
's background -
"There are many rumors about what Seven did to land himself in Lost Whisper, an oubliette for the most dangerous of occultists. (...) wards designed to not just prevent mystical interference with the execution, but to simultaneously obliterate Seven's soul so that it could never be contacted, resurrected, or otherwise be used by someone looking to follow in his footsteps... failed. (...) He laughed as he tasted the fresh air that was denied to him for years."
↑
Newscaster
's in-game
voice line
-
"We were told that Lost Whisper was impregnable. But is there any truth to the rumor that a dangerous inmate escaped? The New York Oracle investigates."
↑
The Binding of Oathkeeper
-
Chapter 3B

lore.title: Maelstrom
lore.content:
The Maelstrom as seen from the
Hideout
.
The eclipse during the Maelstrom.
“
Once, an eclipse changed the world! Who knows what today's celestial event will bring.
— The
Newscaster
A
Maelstrom
is a celestial event that brings the supernatural to the mortal world.
[
1
]
Summary
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Brought about by an eclipse, the first Maelstrom occurred
circa 1899
.
[
2
]
This Maelstrom brought a large influx of spirits back to the mortal world, one of which included
Vindicta
,
[
1
]
and caused some dormant
Astral Gates
to open, including one in New York's Central Park.
[
3
]
[
4
]
This was the event that introduced the general public to the supernatural.
[
5
]
50 years later, in the present day, a second Maelstrom is about to occur. This time, the world is aware that it's coming, and some cults have prepared a ritual to summon the
Patrons
during the celestial event. It's implied that summoning the Patrons outside of the Maelstrom is impossible.
[
6
]
The duration of the Maelstrom is expected to last between 20 minutes to 2 hours,
[
7
]
during which mystic energy is at its apex.
[
8
]
The
OSIC
has warned
New York
citizens to stay indoors for the duration of the Maelstrom.
[
9
]
[
10
]
The third Maelstrom isn't expected to happen for another 50 years,
[
11
]
[
12
]
but it could happen earlier.
[
13
]
Ritual
[
edit
|
edit source
]
The ritual is an event that takes place during the second Maelstrom, in which two
teams
of six (or less) individuals are fighting to summon a
Patron
,
[
14
]
[
10
]
an
extraplanar
elder god,
[
15
]
[
11
]
who can grant them a wish. The ritual can only be completed once the enemy team's Patron is destroyed.
Fonts of power all over
New York City
are being tapped as part of the ritual.
[
16
]
The New York Oracle
is providing live coverage of the ritual.
[
17
]
Haze
has prepared extensively for the ritual by performing drills, research, stretching, divination and fortune telling.
[
18
]
Warden
has trained his whole life for the ritual.
[
19
]
Trivia
[
edit
|
edit source
]
The in-game skybox has an eclipse over the sun. The eclipse was removed with the skybox change in
October 24, 2024
, and added back with the new skybox in
February 25, 2025
.
References
[
edit
|
edit source
]
↑
1.0
1.1
Vindicta
's background -
"Vindicta got a second chance of life when her spirit was brought back to the mortal plane during the first Maelstrom (the event that introduced the supernatural to the public at large)."
↑
Newscaster
's in-game
voice line
-
"Once, an eclipse changed the world! Who knows what today's celestial event will bring?"
↑
Dynamo
's background -
"As a Scientist, Professor Dynamo always had an inquisitive mind. So when a rift in space and time opened up in central park he was first on the scene to investigate"
↑
Warden
's background -
"When the portal opened in Central Park, Warden’s parents realized not only was everything they were told was true, they were unprepared."
↑
Warden
's background -
"Most people think the supernatural came into the world 50 years ago. They’re wrong. Its presence shaped folklore for generations…"
↑

's in-game

↑

10.1

's in-game

↑

11.1

:

↑
Dynamo

↑

's in-game

↑

lore.title: Magical creatures
lore.content:
Aside from the
playable characters
and
minor characters
, there are plenty of magical creatures that exist in the world of
Deadlock
. Many creatures arrived when the
Astral Gates
opened, some evolved from regular creatures due to the large influx of magic, and some creatures were present on Earth long before the first
Maelstrom
.
Elaborated creatures
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Lycanthropes
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Silver
is a known lycanthrope, specifically a werewolf. She claims to have control over her lycanthropy,
[
1
]
although it's shown in-game that she actually can't control the transformation.
[
2
]
Silver has wolf ears and a tail in her human form, and it's currently unknown if this is common amongst werewolves. There is a headline in the
New York Oracle
about looking out for signs of Lycanthropy in your neighbours.
[
3
]
On top of the wolf ears and tail, Silver also has a heightened sense of smell in her human form.
[
4
]
[
5
]
According to Silver, wereotters exist.
[
6
]
Werewolf meat is currently a popular cuisine in France, causing controversy in New York over whether or not it's cannibalism.
[
7
]
Mole men
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Mole men were created by the influx of magic from the first Maelstrom, evolving from regular moles into mole men. A portrait of mole men's evolution can be seen in the
Hideout
.
Mo
is a known mole man. While Mo is mute outside of
grunting during his ult
, Krill implies that Maurice can speak normally.
[
8
]
[
9
]
Mole men are known to be strong,
[
10
]
[
11
]
having the ability to dig through concrete with ease.
[
12
]
Spirits
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Unlike souls, spirits seem to be fully sentient, some being the spectral form of a deceased person.
Sinclair
and
Vindicta
are known spirits.
Some people that died a long time ago were brought back to the living realm during the first Maelstrom as ghosts, such as Vindicta and
Catherine the Great
.
[
13
]
[
14
]
Other people, such as Sinclair (being Henry and Savanna), became spirits due to a blood pact binding their souls together, preventing them from moving on from the living realm.
[
15
]
It's implied that not all ghosts have their own bodies, instead opting to possess people or objects. Sinclair seems to be possessing a wooden mannequin, based on sound of their
footsteps
. An 8-year-old concert pianist was suspected to be possessed by an 18th-century composer.
[
16
]
There was a trial regarding a botched exorcism which caused the spirit "inhuman suffering."
[
17
]
It's implied that modern magic can be used to raise spirits from the dead.
[
18
]
Resurrerecting someone back to their mortal body seems to be possible, as
Raven
has been searching for a way to make Catherine the Great corporeal again.
[
14
]
Not all spirits can interact with the living, as some graveyards are full of spirits who have unfinished business or demands left in the living world. As a necromancer,
Graves
can communicate with the silent dead with the help of her lich mentor.
[
19
]
Her job involves helping give the dead peace.
[
20
]
Graves views her role as a voice for the dead and considers it an honor.
[
21
]
Vindicta calls herself a ghost for "simplicity's sake".
[
22
]
Unicorns
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Despite magic being known worldwide, unicorns are still widely believed to be mythical, non-existent creatures.
[
23
]
Celeste
is a known unicorn.
Unicorns are implied to be extremely powerful.
[
24
]
[
25
]
Celeste is hiding her identity as a unicorn out of fear of getting poached for her horn.
[
23
]
It's currently unknown if unicorns look human, or if they look like horses and Celeste is putting on a disguise. Celeste's model has tattoos of horseshoes and unicorns across her body. Celeste also mentions that she's "supposed to be found in the woods".
[
26
]
Vampires
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Vampires existed on Earth long before the first Maelstrom.
[
27
]
Most vampires live in their own society, known as "The Vampire Nation", which Drifter describes as a "Ponzi Scheme".
[
28
]
They follow their own laws and traditions.
[
29
]
Known vampires include
Drifter
and
Mina
. Drifter has been a vampire for centuries,
[
30
]
while Mina has been a vampire for less than 5 years.
[
31
]
Deadlock's vampires seem to possess similar abilities to many vampires in fiction, such as immortality/eternal youth.
[
32
]
Additionally, both Mina and Drifter express control over blood (as both use it as ammunition for their weapons).
The older vampires, also known as "vampire elders",
[
33
]
hold their archiac titles in high regard, which Drifter considers "soft".
[
34
]
The Vampire Nation doesn't care about human clout, as Mina is at the bottom of the vampiric social ladder despite her wealth and fame.
[
35
]
[
36
]
Vampires seem unwilling to turn humans into vampires, as it cost Mina's parents a small fortune to find a single vampire willing to turn Mina.
[
37
]
Vampires are a protected species in America,
[
38
]
although the
Venators
of the Vatican are known for hunting vampires.
[
39
]
[
40
]
The vampire elders and viscounts want Drifter dead for his refusal to follow their laws.
[
41
]
Zephyrs
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Also known as "wind spirits", zephyrs are used to power airships for public travel, although there are concerns about putting people's safety in the hands of zephyrs.
[
42
]
Holliday
refuses to use airships.
[
43
]
Zephyr powered airships are used instead of aeroplanes.
[
44
]
Mentioned creatures
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Familiars
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Calico
and
Vindicta
have familiars: Calico has her cat, Ava,
[
45
]
and Vindicta has her crow.
[
46
]
There is a cat café run by familiars "Peppermint" and "Mittens" in SoHo.
[
47
]
There is also a shelter for stray familiars in SoHo.
[
48
]
Rem
is referred to as a familiar.
[
49
]
Gargoyles
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Gargoyles are present throughout
New York
, however
Ivy
is the only known gargoyle to have gained life. How Ivy came to life is unknown.
[
50
]
Golems
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Created with a mixture of craftsmanship, willpower and sacrifice,
[
51
]
golems are built to fulfil some kind of duty.
[
52
]
Bebop
is a known golem, nicknamed as "the scrap golem".
[
53
]
[
54
]
It's implied that golems are usually created with bindings on them to prevent them from gaining free will, although Bebop was created without any restrictions.
[
55
]
Liches
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Graves
' weapon and mentor is a lich's hand. Despite being a severed hand, the lich is still able to communicate with Graves. The lich allows Graves to communicate with the dead and summon corpses during the ritual.
[
19
]
[
56
]
While the general nature of liches are unknown, they're implied to be untrustworthy.
[
57
]
[
58
]
[
59
]
Loch Ness Monster
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Also known as "Nessie", the Loch Ness Monster came to Earth through the Astral Gate in
The Deep
.
[
60
]
Rats
[
edit
|
edit source
]
While not a traditionally magical creature, the rats of New York were enhanced by the Maelstrom, causing them to become even more difficult to deal with.
[
61
]
Undead
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Similar yet different to spirits, the undead are reanimated corpses.
Kelvin
,
[
62
]
Victor
[
63
]
and potentially
Seven
are undead.
[
64
]
Yetis
[
edit
|
edit source
]
There is a yeti who's part of the "Yukon Lumberjack Show," who's entertained Canadian audiences for years and are going on tour to America.
[
65
]
Gallery
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Portrait of Mole Men's evolution in the hideout.
References
[
edit
|
edit source
]

lore.title: Maximilian Fairfax
lore.content:
Maximilian Fairfax in
The Binding of Oathkeeper.
“
- You are a man with powerful enemies.
- I have enemies. "Powerful" is a bit generous.
—
Lady Geist
and Maximilian Fairfax
Maximilian Fairfax
is the CEO of
Fairfax Industries
, and the father of
Pocket
and Pocket's siblings.
Background
[
edit
|
edit source
]
He is a white middle-aged bald man with a brown beard. He has a large, muscular build, and is seen wearing a gray suit.
[
1
]
He may have ordered
Pocket's
assassination,
[
2
]
which lead Pocket to go into hiding. He does not fully believe that Pocket is deceased,
[
1
]
and is after their magic suitcase.
[
3
]
Calico
says Maximilian "will have questions" when she kills Pocket,
[
4
]
implying that she was the assassin hired to kill Pocket.
As the CEO of Fairfax Industries, Maximilian is the employer of
McGinnis
and
Wrecker
. Maximilian "paraded McGinnis incessantly" to the
New York
elite when he first discovered her as a genius teenage inventor.
[
5
]
Maximilian was an acquaintance of
Professor Dynamo
, who is Pocket's godfather.
[
6
]
Maximilian with
Oathkeeper
in his arm.
According to
Haze
and
McGinnis
, Maximilian values his legacy more than his money.
[
7
]
Maximilian donated "a small fortune" to New York orphanages in December. He said to the
New York Oracle
that "no one should be without family on the holidays".
[
8
]
Paradox
claims to have blackmail on Fairfax, which will be exhibited in the Paradox Gallery.
[
9
]
A possible ending for
The Binding of Oathkeeper
has
Lady Geist
transferring
Oathkeeper
to Maximilian. Maximilian wants to "change the world" with Oathkeeper's help.
[
1
]
References
[
edit
|
edit source
]
↑
1.0
1.1
1.2
The Binding of Oathkeeper
-
Chapter 5C
↑
Pocket
's in-game
voice line
-
"My father's gift for my 18th birthday was a bullet to the chest. Today is my chance to say 'thank you'."
↑
Pocket
's in-game
voice line
-
"I'll do whatever it takes to keep this case from my father."
↑
Calico
's in-game
voice line
(killed Pocket) -
"I'm sure Maximilian will have questions."
↑
The Binding of Oathkeeper
-
Chapter 3A
↑
Dynamo and Pocket conversation
↑
Haze and McGinnis conversation
- Haze:
"When you're someone who cares about legacy as much as he does, that's probably more valuable than money."
McGinnis:
"Exactly."
↑
Newscaster
's in-game
voice line
-
"Newscaster voice line - This month, Maximilian Fairfax made headlines after donating a small fortune to New York orphanages. When asked about his generous act, the CEO of Fairfax Industries replied: 'No one should be without family on the holidays'."
↑
Paradox and Wraith conversation

lore.title: Minor characters
lore.content:
Aside from the
playable characters
in the game, the setting of
Deadlock
also features a series of
minor characters
, usually mentioned in headlines from
The New York Oracle
, the backgrounds of heroes or the visual novels.
Direct Relations
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Ms. Shelly Fisher
Shelly Fisher
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Main article:
Shelly Fisher
Shelly Fisher
is
Bebop
's creator. She is a widowed junkyard attendant who built Bebop, thus relying on Bebop for support in paying for her treatment.
Johann Geist Jr.
is her doctor.
[
1
]
The Geists
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Johann Geist
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Born in 1805 and dying in 1880,
Johann Geist
was a German painter and the husband of
Jeanne Geist
.
[
1
]
50 years before the
Ritual
, the
Paradox Organisation
stole one of Johann's paintings.
[
2
]
[
3
]
[
4
]
Walter Geist
[
edit
|
edit source
]
The son of Johann and
Jeanne Geist
. Born in 1850 and died sometime in the 1900s.
[
1
]
Johann Geist (Jr.)
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Main article:
Johann Geist Jr.
The grandson of
Lady Geist
and the doctor of
Shelly Fisher
.
[
1
]
The Arroyos
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Main article:
The Arroyo's
Carlos Arroyo
[
edit
|
edit source
]
A Community organiser in Spanish Harlem. Possibly looking to run for mayor.
[
5
]
Sophia Arroyo
[
edit
|
edit source
]
A young woman who studies under
Dynamo
in Columbia University. She works with
Wraith
to fund her tuition.
[
5
]
Manny Arroyo
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Sophia's younger brother. He's a fan of
Kelvin
and his books.
[
6
]
[
7
]
Marla
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Marla is
Professor Dynamo's
wife. They have been married for 31 years.
[
8
]
She bakes Dynamo key lime pies despite him being incapable of eating them and made enough for Dynamo to share with his team if they win.
[
9
]
[
10
]
Agent Marlowe
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Main article:
OSIC
Marlowe is an
OSIC
Agent.
[
11
]
[
12
]
He also is a friend of
Venator
.
The Troubadour
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Main article:
The Troubadour
"The Troubadour"
is the name given by media for a serial killer who commited a killing spree in a town in central Illinois. He was confronted by the town's sheriff,
Holliday
, but he managed to escape by turning into a murder of crows and flying away, although Holliday still has been looking for him. Both she and the
New York Oracle
speculate that The Troubadour is flying from
Champaign–Urbana
to New York.
[
11
]
Nashala Dion
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Nashala Dion
is a Djinn ambassador, who has employed
Mirage
to work as her bodyguard and companion. Mirage enjoys Nashala's company, but he also finds her "impulsive, short-sighted" decisions "frustrating".
[
13
]
She (and Mirage) are currently in New York attempting to arrange a deal so the Djinn can buy a portion of
Wyoming
.
Cadence and the Requiem Boys
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Main article:
Cadence
Cadence and the Requiem Boys
are a band, with
Cadence
as their lead vocalist, that produced the single "Paying the Price". The song has concerned some parents over its mention and "glorification" of human sacrifice.
[
14
]
John Hathorne
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Wikipedia article:
John Hathorne
John Hathorne
was a magistrate and merchant who had a key role as a judge in the
Salem Witch Trials
during the 1600s which persecuted people believed to be witches. One of the people sentenced to death by him was
Vindicta
, who now holds a grudge against his descendants after she was brought back into reality from the first
maelstrom
.
John Hathorne (Jr.)
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Main article:
Friends of Humanity
A "controversial speaker" and descendant of the original John Hathorne currently in New York.
[
15
]
He is a member of the neo inquisition political group "
Friends of Humanity
" which has rallied for the US to stop sending aid to the
Outer Planes
.
There was an assassination attempt on his life.
[
16
]
Ettrick
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Ettrick is an old friend of
Kelvin
, who the latter has not seen since his return from the expedition. Ettrick held ambitions for an unknown goal, which drove Kelvin to push him away.
[
17
]
Ettrick was "rather rude" and "caused a scene" at Kelvin's fundraiser, according to
Lady Geist
.
[
18
]
The Adversary
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Main article:
The Deep
The Adversary is a creature from an
Outer Plane
that stalks
the Deep
. It is described as "dark and predatory" and "a genocidal force of nature".
Viscous
says that if the Adversary isn't stopped, "he will come for everything".
[
19
]
Captain Murphy
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Captain Murphy is
Holliday
's contact in the New York Police Department. She uses his help to track down The Troubadour.
[
11
]
Yamato
threatens to kill Murphy should Holliday not "play her part" during the ritual.
[
20
]
Allie Kinkaid
[
edit
|
edit source
]
A reporter from The New York Oracle. She's writing an interview with
Seven
.
[
11
]
[
21
]
Hank
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Main article:
Jezebel's
Hank is the owner of
Jezebel's Billard Hall
. He's the boss and close friend of
Infernus
and is friends with
Lady Geist
.
[
1
]
Bryce
[
edit
|
edit source
]
The brother of
Paige
, Bryce was a rebellious teen who wanted to use his family's bibliomancy for his own desires. A few years before the events of Deadlock, he was kicked out of his family's house for unknown reasons, and has been missing since. Paige is participating in the ritual to find out what happened to Bryce and to restore her family.
[
22
]
Bryce used to hang out with
Vyper
[
23
]
and is described by Paige as a "curmudgeon".
[
24
]
Venator
believes Bryce is beyond saving, expressing remorse that Paige has "thrown away her life to save someone who doesn't deserve it".
[
25
]
[
26
]
Haze
believes Paige would be happier if she lets her brother go.
[
27
]
Molly Molenzo
[
edit
|
edit source
]
A convict at Rikers, Molly was shanked by
Vyper
using a toothbrush-mattress spring combo.
[
28
]
The stabbing of Molly was heard about by
Wraith
, who mentions that she "isn't friends" with Molly.
[
29
]
Yamato
wants to speak to Molly about something important.
[
30
]
Blockhead Lenny
[
edit
|
edit source
]
One of the sideshows at Coney Island, Lenny is friends and coworkers with
Celeste
. He's implied to be stubborn,
[
31
]
and is possibly the friend of Celeste who sold part of their soul to cover a gambling debt.
[
32
]
[
33
]
Lenny once got beaten by
Lash
in the
Bear Pit
.
[
34
]
Part of Lenny's act includes knife throwing.
[
35
]
Keruba the Mermaid, Chickenwing and Ted
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Friends of
Celeste
who work alongside her at Coney Island's sideshow.
[
32
]
Mentioned Characters
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Characters mentioned in passing by
The New York Oracle
.
Harry Houdini
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Wikipedia article:
Harry Houdini
Rumoured to still be alive and possibly evaded death itself.
[
36
]
Michael Assan
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Wrote "A Memoir by Incubus to the Stars" which spent 3 weeks on the top of The New York Oracle's best seller list.
[
37
]
Madame Lapri
[
edit
|
edit source
]
A relationships expert who wrote a column in The New York Oracle about love potions.
[
38
]
Nathan Vice
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Centre for the Knickerbockers, had their legs broken by The Curiosity Shop shopkeep for one of the items.
[
39
]
Rodgers and Hammerstein
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Wikipedia article:
Rodgers and Hammerstein
A duo of theater writers who produced the musical South Pacific, which had its tickets scalped and resold for 1/8 of a soul.
[
40
]
Eric Clansky
[
edit
|
edit source
]
A man standing trial for a botched exorcism.
[
41
]
Miser Heart
[
edit
|
edit source
]
The person accusing Mr Clansky in court for causing the spirit inhuman pain and suffering.
George Frederick Cooke
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Wikipedia article:
George Frederick Cooke
Still performing on stage but as a headless ghost.
[
42
]
Mittens and Peppermint
[
edit
|
edit source
]
A cat familiar duo running a cat café in Soho.
[
43
]
Trivia
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Allie Kinkaid's name may be a reference to
Alex Kincaide
, a journalist character in Valve's
Counter-Strike
:
Global Offensive
.
Chickenwing and Ted were both teased 3 months before Celeste's backstory was revealed, in a joke post by developer
Hopoo
.
[
44
]

lore.title: Municipal Coven
lore.content:
“
The city owes you boys in the Municipal Coven a lot.
—
Shopkeeper
The Municipal Coven makes sure the streets of New York stay safe and tidy.
Summary
[
edit
|
edit source
]
The New York Municipal Coven works as the primary caretakers of New York, with
Trapper
being a known employee there. The Municipal Coven's duties include building Spirit Urns, luring giant rats, and capturing invasive species from other realms.
[
1
]
Trivia
[
edit
|
edit source
]
The full name of the Municipal Coven is the "New York Municipal Coven".
[
1
]
Staff were given the option to work on Maelstrom day, which
Trapper
volunteered to do.
[
2
]
Trapper believes the Municipal Coven needs to be respected more.
[
3
]
Holliday
[
4
]
and the
Shopkeeper
[
5
]
respect what the Municipal Coven does for the city.
The Municipal Coven seems to accept donations.
[
6
]
During the festive season, the Municipal Coven were looking for volunteers to spend time with stray familiars in their Soho shelter.
[
7
]
References
[
edit
|
edit source
]
↑
1.0
1.1

's background:
"An employee of the New York Municipal Coven; Trapper spends his days building Spirit Urns, luring giant rats, and capturing various invasive species from The Outer Planes."
↑
Trapper
's select
voice line
:
"Why did I volunteer to work Maelstrom day?"
↑
Trapper
's select
voice line
:
"More people need to start respecting the Municipal Coven."
↑
Holliday
's in-game
voice line
:
"I got nothin' against the boys in the Coven, but you're not stopping me!"
↑
Shopkeeper
's in-game
voice line
:
"The city owes you boys in the Municipal Coven a lot."
↑
Pocket
's in-game
voice line
:
"I'll give a donation to the Municipal Coven later."
↑
Newscaster's seasonal headline 22
:
"The New York Municipal Coven is looking for volunteers to spend time with stray familiars in their Soho shelter. "

lore.title: Oathkeeper
lore.content:
Oathkeeper's true form in
The Binding of Oathkeeper
.
“
Oathkeeper wasn't named ironically; that thing is like the Patron Saint of
Faustian Bargains
.
— Johann Geist Jr.
Oathkeeper
is a spirit residing in
Lady Geist
's arm that provides her with power and a youthful appearance in return for feeding on the life force of others.
Summary
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Circa 1899
, the first
Maelstrom
happened, bringing various supernatural forces to Earth. Around this time, Lady Geist was an old lady stuck in a nursing home, dreaming of her glorious past. At this moment, Oathkeeper revealed himself to Geist with a proposition: he could restore her youth; all she had to do was drain the essence of the living to sustain herself.
[
1
]
Understanding that Oathkeeper likely couldn't be trusted, Lady Geist had a ward built to bind Oathkeeper to her arm shortly after their deal was made. The purpose of the ward was to limit Oathkeeper's influence over Geist, keeping him under control. As the ward was "against the spirit of the bargain," Oathkeeper promised to make Geist suffer if he ever broke free.
[
2
]
Since that day, Oathkeeper has been bound to Geist for roughly 50 years.
[
3
]
However, the ward has started to weaken, and it won't be long until he successfully breaks the ward and is released from his prison.
[
1
]
Lady Geist's visual novel,
The Binding of Oathkeeper
, tells of Lady Geist's story to prevent Oathkeeper from breaking free and killing her. This is also Lady Geist's motivation for participating in the ritual.
[
4
]
[
5
]
Oathkeeper's power comes from him feeding on life. The more youthful and "quality" the life is, the longer Oathkeeper is satiated.
[
2
]
If he is starved, his power drains, which causes Lady Geist to return to her old age. Apparently, Oathkeeper's hunger has become harder to satiate, as a year ago Geist could kill one vagrant to stay youthful throughout Summer,
[
6
]
yet in the present day killing three vagrants would only satiate Oathkeeper for 2 months.
[
7
]
It's possible for Oathkeeper to be transferred from Lady Geist to a different host, as seen in The Binding of Oathkeeper where Lady Geist pawns off Oathkeeper to
Maximilian Fairfax
.
[
8
]
The ward binding Oathkeeper was originally constructed by Lady Geist's son,
Walter Geist
, who did extensive research into Oathkeeper.
[
9
]
[
10
]
The resposibilities of maintaining and upgrading the ward after Walter's death fell to his son,
Johann Geist Jr
, who last upgraded the ward 5 years ago.
[
11
]
According to Lady Geist, the ward acts like a puzzle box that Oathkeeper would be compelled to open, which diverts his attention away from making demands of Geist.
[
12
]
The reason why the ward is failing is because Oathkeeper "got better at multitasking". It isn't possible for Johann Jr. to make a better ward.
[
13
]
Aside from keeping Oathkeeper under control, the ward also prevents Oathkeeper from interacting with the world outside his prison. Lady Geist can choose when she wants Oathkeeper to see or listen to the outside world.
[
14
]
Only Lady Geist can hear Oathkeeper speak, with the main exception of
Graves
.
[
15
]
Oathkeeper originated from a different realm, coming to Earth after being conjured by the tome
Detective Abrams
carries.
[
16
]
While it's unknown when Oathkeeper was summoned, he is old friends with
Drifter
and apparently goes "way back" with him,
[
17
]
implying that Oathkeeper was on Earth before the First Maelstrom.
Oathkeeper is described as a "parasite" who needs a host to survive.
[
18
]
Trivia
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Oathkeeper is voiced by Matthew Mercer in the visual novel.
References
[
edit
|
edit source
]
↑
1.0
1.1
Lady Geist
's background
↑
2.0
2.1
The Binding of Oathkeeper
-
Chapter 3c
↑
The Binding of Oathkeeper
-
Chapter 1
-
Johann Jr.
:
"How long have you had Oathkeeper trapped in your arm? Thirty years?" -
Lady Geist
:
"Closer to f... 50."
↑

's in-game
voice line
(Lady Geist start):
"Time is running out, Lady Geist. Summon the Hidden King before Oathkeeper breaks free."
↑
Lady Geist
's in-game

"Soon I'll be free of you, Oathkeeper."
↑
The Binding of Oathkeeper
-
Chapter 3b
- Oathkeeper:
"My power-" -

:
"Is weakening. You think I haven't noticed? Last year, I could kill a vagrant and enjoy the rest of swimsuit season. Now the same hobo would barely last me a drive to The Hamptons."
↑
