lore.title: Occult Security and Investigation Commission
lore.content:
OSIC letter addressed to
Dynamo
“
You know it's bad when they send a Sandman.
—
Haze
A secretive "Black Box"
United States
government organization, the
Occult Security and Investigation Commission
(
OSIC
, for short) specializes in keeping the country safe from sinister and supernatural forces.
[
1
]
Summary
[
edit
|
edit source
]
The Occult Security and Investigation Commission works hard to ensure supernatural threats that are too big for local law enforcement to handle are taken care of.
[
1
]
The OSIC keeps files on many notable persons,
[
2
]
and once a threat to national security has been identified, a Sandman is sent to neutralize the threat.
[
3
]
The OSIC has warned
New York
citizens to stay indoors for the duration of the
Maelstrom
.
[
4
]
Haze
has been sent to fight in the
ritual
by the OSIC, although the details of her mission are currently unknown.
[
5
]
[
6
]
The OSIC wants
Calico
and
Seven
dead.
[
7
]
[
8
]
They seem to know that Seven is in New York, as Seven is hiding from them in New York's subway tunnels.
[
9
]
The OSIC is also keeping an eye on
Wraith
and
Yamato
,
[
10
]
[
11
]
although they haven't been considered worth allocating resources to take out yet. Haze mentions that if the
Seventh Moon
becomes a cartel, then her superiors would likely send her to kill Yamato.
[
12
]
The OSIC doesn't seem to be after
Drifter
.
[
13
]
The OSIC is implied to be worried about creatures of
The Deep
.
[
14
]
They seem to know about the
Adversary
and are aware of how dangerous it is.
[
15
]
Once the ritual is over, Haze wants
Viscous
to tell her everything about the Adversary so the OSIC can prepare in case any more creatures like it come through The Deep's
Astral Gate
.
[
16
]
The OSIC's main office is not local to New York. Instead, they've set up a temporary base of operations in the area, although Haze believes that the OSIC will open a permanent office after the ritual due to all the activity going on in the area.
[
17
]
Haze acknowledges that the OSIC is flawed and that they don't have enough people "who care what doors are getting kicked down".
[
18
]
[
19
]
That said, she is still loyal to her job and believes that the ends justify the means.
[
20
]
Haze works for the OSIC because she "loves her country".
[
21
]
Lady Geist
is worried she will be sent to an OSIC oubliette if she caters to
Oathkeeper
's demands.
[
22
]
Haze warns her that the OSIC will have to intervene if "the Oathkeeper issue isn't resolved".
[
23
]
The OSIC is interested in
Kelvin
's condition as a revenant.
[
24
]
They also show some interest in
Celeste
being a unicorn.
[
25
]
The OSIC has released a report about hexes.
[
26
]
The OSIC limits expeditions to the North Pole.
[
27
]
Members
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Haze
- OSIC agent in the Sandman Division
Marlowe - OSIC agent
Agent Marlowe
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Marlowe is an agent for the OSIC. While his exact role in the organization is unknown, he's noted as someone who "spins stories" and seems to be one of Haze's superiors.
[
28
]
[
29
]
Haze mentions that Marlowe made "a good offer" to
Dynamo
. While the details of the offer are unclear, Dynamo refuses it, although Marlowe told Haze that the offer still stands in case Dynamo changes his mind.
[
30
]
Dynamo carries a letter from the OSIC addressed to him in his bag.
Venator
is a friend of Marlowe and calls him a "bastard".
[
28
]
Haze believes Marlowe would want to know about
The Doorman
's involvement in the ritual.
[
31
]
Marlowe makes an appearance in
Holliday
's
visual novel
, where he tells Holliday and
Captain Murphy
that the OSIC is taking over the investigation into the Troubadour. He asks Holliday to either help him or get out of his way.
[
32
]
While his role in the story past chapter 2 is unclear, Holliday's conversations with Seven imply that his goal was to hire the Troubadour.
[
33
]
Divisions
[
edit
|
edit source
]
The OSIC has multiple divisions. Known divisions include:
The Sandman Division
The Curator Division
The Medium Division
Sandman Division
[
edit
|
edit source
]
A division specialized in infiltrating dreams.
Haze
is a notable member of this division; seeking out the dreams of "persons of interest" to investigate them. If necessary, members of the Sandman Division have clearance to "eliminate" significant detected threats.
[
1
]
The OSIC's Sandmen rarely work together.
[
34
]
It's unknown how many Sandmen there are, but Haze says that there's enough of them to call themselves a division.
[
35
]
The OSIC has not confirmed or denied the existence of the Sandman Division.
[
36
]
The Sandman Division has released a holiday calendar to "soften their image", but it only made them seem "more terrifying".
[
37
]
Curator Division
[
edit
|
edit source
]
The Curator Division deals with magical objects like
Pocket
's case or
Graves
' lich hand.
[
38
]
[
39
]
According to
Haze
, "Curators care about things and Sandmen care about people".
[
3
]
Haze believes
Paige
would be a good Curator.
[
40
]
Paradox
wants Haze to put in a good word with the Curator Division for her.
[
41
]
Medium Division
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Not much is known about the Medium Division.
Haze
recommends a job as an OSIC medium to
Graves
, asking her to consider the offer and call her if she's interested.
[
42
]
Trivia
[
edit
|
edit source
]
OSIC door in the work-in-progress Hideout map
The initialism OSIC is pronounced "oh-ess-i-see".
Haze
's background compares the OSIC to the
CIA
, which was founded in 1947 in the real world. The CIA is also mentioned in
Wrecker
's background.
In an older work-in-progress version of the
Hideout
map, a door labeled "O.S.I.C. Restricted Area" could be found.
A removed
Dynamo
voiceline suggested that Sandmen were considered a conspiracy theory.

lore.title: Outer Planes
lore.content:
An advert for
Astral Plane Canned Mille-Pattes Dazzle
The Outer Planes
refer to planes of existence beyond the known universe.
Some Outer Planes are connected to Earth by
Astral Gates
.
Summary
[
edit
|
edit source
]
The Outer Planes include
Ixia
[
1
]
and the
Patron's
plane.
[
2
]
Some souls of humans move to an Outer Plane after death.
[
3
]
The US is currently sending aid to the Outer Planes due to the military campaign in Ixia.
[
4
]
[
1
]
The Doorman
is an extraplanar being and an "old friend" of the Patrons.
[
5
]
[
6
]
An exhibit at the Met is due to open up showing off artifacts from the Outer Planes.
[
7
]
The
New York
Astral Gate
has led to extraplanar invasive species appearing in the city.
[
8
]
Astral Plane Canned Mille-Pattes Dazzle
("mille-pattes" is French for "centipede") are advertised as
The World's Most Popular Delicacy
.
References
[
edit
|
edit source
]
↑
1.0
1.1
2024-09-03 -
Yoshi
; Deadlock Discord -
"Ixia is one of the outer planes that is connected to our world via an astral gate. That is the plane that characters that we refer to as 'demons' would have historical roots in. That being said though, at this time in the world of Deadlock there are plenty of Ixians that are born on Earth: Infernus and Grey Talon's daughter-in-law (RIP). There are military operations happening in Ixia right now."
↑
Newscaster
's in-game
voice line
-
"The orders of The Amber Hand and Sapphire Flame have long provided safeguards insuring that The Patrons remain on their home plane. But have the people we trusted to be wardens of The Patrons secretly been planning a coup?"
↑
Vindicta
's background -
"Vindicta got a second chance of life when her spirit was brought back to the mortal plane during the first Maelstrom (the event that introduced the supernatural to the public at large)."
↑
Newscaster
's in-game
voice line
-
"As the military campaign in Ixia continues, the controversial political organization Friends of Humanity announced a rally in New York to demand the US cease sending aid to the Outer Planes."
↑
The Doorman
's background -
"While he had only planned to experience life on this plane for a few weeks, The Doorman quickly discovered that the New York service industry was a great way to see the strange demands that please humans."
↑
The Doorman
's in-game
voice line
-
"It was so nice getting a chance to summon old friends."
↑
Newscaster
's in-game
voice line
-
"'Artifacts of the Outer Planes': A new exhibit at the Met is gearing up to open next month. Read all about it in The New York Oracle."
↑
Trapper
's background -
"Trapper spends his days building Spirit Urns, luring giant rats, and capturing various invasive species from The Outer Planes."

lore.title: Paradox Museum
lore.content:
“
Think I'll take a walk through the museum.
—
Paradox
The Paradox Museum is a pop-up gallery showcasing everything the Paradox Organisation steals.
Summary
[
edit
|
edit source
]
The Paradox Organisation
steals priceless artifacts to be showcased at the Paradox Museum, with the intention of showing that anything can be stolen by Paradox.
[
1
]
Once a sufficient number of artifacts are gathered, the museum is open to the public for a short amount of time.
[
2
]
The Paradox Museum isn't exclusively for art: it's also used to showcase stolen state secrets, celebrity paternity tests,
[
1
]
and forms of blackmail on renowned individuals.
[
3
]
As a pop-up gallery, the Paradox Museum has no set location.
[
1
]
Where Paradox stores their artifacts while the Museum is closed is unknown.
Trivia
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Characters that have visited the Paradox Museum include but aren't limited to
Calico
,
[
4
]
Dynamo
[
5
]
and
Lady Geist
.
[
6
]
Vyper
has also visited the museum, but was kicked out.
[
7
]
The items that are known to be showcased at the Paradox Museum include
The Ghent Altarpiece
,
[
3
]
a
hellenistic Bust of Homer
,
[
8
]
one of
Johann Geist's
paintings,
[
9
]
[
10
]
the Tome of Anubis (which
Raven
allegedly also has),
[
3
]
[
11
]
and some kind of "blackmail on Fairfax".
[
3
]
Calico
and
Dynamo
both show adoration for the Paradox Museum.
[
5
]
[
4
]
Lady Geist
, on the other hand, describes it as "a multi-million exhibition with the emotional depth of a 10th grader writing "I will rage against conformity" in a poetry unit of Remedial English."
[
12
]
References
[
edit
|
edit source
]
↑
1.0
1.1
1.2
Paradox
's background:
"What does Paradox do with the countless artifacts, state secrets, and celebrity paternity tests that they have stolen? Put it on display at pop-up museums, so that they can show the world that no one is out of Paradox’s reach."
↑
Paradox & Wraith Conversation 3
-
Paradox
:
"We're securing a few more items of interest, but hopefully the gallery will be opened shortly."
↑
3.0
3.1
3.2
3.3
Paradox & Wraith Conversation 3
-
Paradox
:
"Blackmail on Fairfax, the Ghent Altarpiece, the Tome of Anubis..."
↑
4.0
4.1
Calico
's in-game
voice line
:
"Love your museums, by the way."
↑
5.0
5.1
Dynamo & Paradox Conversation 2
-
Dynamo
: "I took Marla to the last pop-up. Truly... wonderful things to behold."
↑
Lady Geist & Paradox Conversation 2
-
Lady Geist
:
"You know, I've been to the Paradox museum last summer."
↑
Vyper
's in-game
voice line
:
"Okay, maybe next time, you guys shouldn't kick me out of the Paradox Museum!"
↑
Lady Geist & Paradox Conversation 2
-
Paradox
:
"It had a hellenistic bust of Homer!"
↑
Lady Geist
's in-game
voice line
:
"First my husband, now me. When will Paradox stay out of my life?"
↑
Lady Geist & Paradox Conversation 1
-
Lady Geist
:
"I heard a rumor that you had one of my husband's paintings."
↑
Raven
's select
voice line
:
"I have the Canopic Jar, I have the Tome of Anubis. All I need to do now is summon the Patron."
↑
Lady Geist & Paradox Conversation 2
-
Lady Geist
:
"It was a multi-million exhibition with the emotional depth of a 10th grader writing "I will rage against conformity" in a poetry unit of Remedial English."

lore.title: Paradox Organisation
lore.content:
“
The only thing I expect from Paradox is style over substance.
—
Grey Talon
The Paradox Organisation is a guild of thieves who want to show the world that there's nothing they can't steal.
Summary
[
edit
|
edit source
]
The Paradox Organisation, also known as Paradox for short, is a group of bored rich thieves who steal various objects of interest.
[
1
]
Whatever Paradox steals is presented at the
Paradox Museum
, a pop-up gallery open to the public with the intention of showing the world that Paradox can steal from anyone.
[
2
]
The Paradox Organisation does not exclusively steal artifacts: they also steal state secrets and blackmail on celebrities.
[
2
]
Paradox
(the playable character) is one member of the Paradox Organisation. Each member of Paradox wears a mask and a coat,
[
3
]
[
4
]
and gives up their name to join the organisation.
[
5
]
The members of Paradox work mostly independently, gathering together a few times a year to discuss their thefts.
[
6
]
[
1
]
The thefts of each Paradox member is "based on individual interest and challenge".
[
7
]
The origins of the Paradox Organisation are unknown. Their oldest known theft seems to be one of
Johann Geist's
paintings, which happened 50 years before the events of the game.
[
8
]
[
9
]
Thefts
[
edit
|
edit source
]
The Paradox Organisation has had several artifacts on its radar. Some thefts have been successful, some have yet to be committed, and some have failed.
Paradox has successfully stolen
The Ghent Altarpiece
, the Tome of Anubis, some form of blackmail on
Fairfax
,
[
10
]
a
hellenistic Bust of Homer
,
[
11
]
one of
Johann Geist's
paintings,
[
12
]
[
9
]
Booth's Deringer
,
[
13
]
and the location of
Seven
's safehouse.
[
14
]
The Paradox Organisation has shown interest in robbing
Kelvin
's home during the ritual.
Paradox
, the playable hero, has shown personal interest in
Abrams
' tome,
[
15
]
Holliday
's revolver,
[
16
]
McGinnis
' research and secrets she holds for Fairfax,
[
17
]
[
18
]
Nashala
,
[
19
]
rare books
Paige
may have,
[
20
]
Pocket
's suitcase,
[
21
]
and
Yamato
's sword.
[
22
]
The Paradox Organisation has tried, and failed, to break into the
Baxter Society
's Manor House, although they haven't given up trying.
[
23
]
They have also been unsuccessful at breaking into the
Baroness Hotel
.
[
24
]
The Paradox Organisation keep trying to steal
Celeste
's horn.
[
25
]
Trivia
[
edit
|
edit source
]
The Paradox Organisation is wary of
Abrams
.
[
26
]
Paradox holds
Kelvin
in high regard.
[
27
]
References
[
edit
|
edit source
]
↑
1.0
1.1
Lady Geist & Paradox Conversation 3
-
Paradox
:
"We're a bunch of bored rich people, of course we brag about what we stole."
↑
2.0
2.1
Paradox
's background:
"Paradox.  An artifact of time, and the name of a notorious thieves' guild... one that prides themselves on constructing elaborate heists that target the most untouchable of individuals and institutions.
What does Paradox do with the countless artifacts, state secrets, and celebrity paternity tests that they have stolen?  Put it on display at pop-up museums, so that they can show the world that no one is out of Paradox’s reach."
↑
Paradox
's background:
"Shrouded in mystery, each member of Paradox dons a mask and takes on the organization's name."
↑
Drifter & Paradox Conversation 2
-
Drifter
:
"Well, it's a nice coat."
-
Paradox
:
"All members of Paradox wear one."
↑
Paradox
's in-game
voice line
(Select):
"I gave up my name to join Paradox."
↑
Paradox & Lady Geist Conversation 1
-
Lady Geist
:
"How often do your little social club gather?"
-
Paradox
:
"A few times a year."
↑
Seven & Paradox Conversation 3
-
Seven
:
"Does Paradox take commissions?"
-
Paradox
:
"... No. Our jobs are based on individual interest and challenge."
↑
Lady Geist
's in-game
voice line
(killed Paradox):
"I've waited fifty years for this."
↑
9.0
9.1
Lady Geist & Paradox Conversation 1
-
Lady Geist
:
"I heard a rumor that you had one of my husband's paintings."
↑
Wraith and Paradox Conversation 3
-
Paradox
:
"Blackmail on Fairfax, the Ghent Altarpiece, the Tome of Anubis..."
↑
Lady Geist & Paradox Conversation 2
-
Paradox
:
"It had a hellenistic bust of Homer!"
↑
Lady Geist
's in-game
voice line
(killed Paradox):
"First my husband, now me. When will Paradox stay out of my life?"
↑
Haze & Paradox Conversation 2
↑
Seven & Paradox Conversation 2
-
Paradox
:
"What is it worth it to you to have your safehouses location... Not exposed?"
↑
Paradox
's in-game
voice line
(Killed Abrams):
"The tome will be mine, Abrams!"
↑
Paradox
's in-game
voice line
(Killed Holliday):
"That gun has history, Holliday, I want it!"
↑
Paradox
's in-game
voice line
(Killed McGinnis):
"I want your research, McGinnis."
↑
Paradox
's in-game
voice line
(Killed McGinnis):
"What secrets are you hiding for Fairfax, McGinnis?"
↑
Paradox
's in-game
voice line
(Killed Mirage):
"Tell you what Mirage: Give me the Djinn, and I'll let you walk."
↑
Paradox
's in-game
voice line
(Killed Paige):
"Bet she has some rare books worth stealin'."
↑
Paradox
's in-game
voice line
(Killed Pocket):
"I want that case, Pocket!"
↑
Paradox
's in-game
voice line
(Killed Yamato):
"I can't wait to steal that sword!"
↑
Grey Talon & Paradox Conversation 2
↑
Paradox
's in-game
voice line
(Killed Doorman):
"You can't keep me out of The Baroness forever, Doorman!"
↑
Celeste
's in-game
voice line
(killed Paradox):
"Sick of those assholes trying to steal my horn..."
↑
Abrams & Paradox Conversation 2
-
Paradox
:
"We like secrets and you seem to have a knack for finding them. But a word of advice. Some things aren't meant to be revealed. Stop asking questions about us."
↑
Paradox & Kelvin Conversation 3
-
Paradox
:
"You... Know you're held in high regard by Paradox, right?"

lore.title: Seventh Moon
lore.content:
“
I knew a guy who owed the Seventh Moon a C-Note... Yamato murdered the shit out of him.
—
Vyper
The Seventh Moon are a Japanese criminal organisation led by Yamato.
To make this page easier to read, Yamato (the playable hero) is referred to by her real name:
Kaori
.
Summary
[
edit
|
edit source
]
The Seventh Moon was once a powerful crime family in Japan, led by siblings
Kaori
and Yamato. The two siblings were born into power, and took over the organisation once their father died. With Yamato's dreams of expanding the Seventh Moon to the
United States
, the two siblings made a plan where Kaori would stay in Japan while Yamato would set up an international presence in America. However, several insecure underlings disliked the idea of being led by Kaori in Japan. This resulted in a brutal coup, where Yamato sacrificed himself to ensure Kaori and a small group of loyalists could escape to America. With a fraction of the power she once held, Kaori took up her brother's name and committed herself to rebuilding the Seventh Moon's might in America.
[
1
]
The strength of the Seventh Moon is a fraction of the power it once held in Japan. While the status of the Seventh Moon in Japan is unknown, it's implied to have been dismantled.
[
2
]
Kaori's wish in the ritual is to make the Seventh Moon powerful enough to control New York.
[
3
]
[
4
]
Kaori intends to challenge the other criminal organisations in New York in the Seventh Moon's rise to power.
[
5
]
She is grateful to
Ivy
for dismantling the
Mendoza Syndicate
[
6
]
and sees
Wraith
as her final hurdle to conquering New York.
[
7
]
The Seventh Moon are small enough for the
OSIC
to not bother allocating resources to dismantle the gang, but they are keeping an eye on things in case the Seventh Moon gets too strong.
[
8
]
[
9
]
Members
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Yamato - Deceased leader
Kaori
(now named Yamato) - Leader
Yamato: The Brother
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Kaori's brother Yamato (not to be confused with the playable Yamato) was one of the co-leaders of the Seventh Moon before he lost his life saving Kaori from a coup. The goal of expanding the Seventh Moon to America was Yamato's dream.
[
1
]
[
10
]
Kaori's ultimate ability (Shadow Transformation) is Kaori infusing Yamato's shadow soul with herself.
[
11
]
[
12
]
According to Kaori, Yamato had a "useless" body guard.
[
13
]
Trivia
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Seventh Moon in Japanese Katakana is spelt "セブンスムーン".
Vyper
is the only hero who shows any interest in joining the Seventh Moon.
[
14
]
References
[
edit
|
edit source
]
↑
1.0
1.1
Yamato
's background
↑
The Doorman
's in-game
voice line
(Hotel Guest Taunt):
"Your brother died to save your life, Kaori, and what do you have to show for it? A life of exile in a country full of people that can't be bothered to learn your real name, and an organization that is a shadow of its former glory."
↑
Yamato
's in-game
voice line
(Select):
"New York will fall into the hands of the Seventh Moon."
↑
Patron
's in-game
voice line
(Yamato Start):
"This is not your city, Yamato, but after you summon me, it will be."
↑
Holliday
And
Yamato Conversation 3
-
"Picking a fight with the other families isn't gonna end well for you"
-
"We'll see."
↑
Yamato
's in-game
voice line
(Killed Ivy):
"Thanks for taking care of Mendoza."
↑
Yamato
's in-game

lore.title: Shelly Fisher
lore.content:
Ms. Shelly in
The Binding of Oathkeeper
“
If you knew anything about me, you'd know I traffic in creation, not death.
— Ms. Shelly
Shelly Fisher
, also known as
Ms. Shelly
or
Miss Shelly
, is a widowed junk yard attendant. She created
Bebop
to assist her in her work and to be the son she could never have.
[
1
]
Summary
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Shelly Fisher is an elderly black woman with grey hair and glasses.
[
2
]
Ms. Shelly has fallen ill and
Bebop
has turned to pit fighting in order to pay for her medication. While Ms. Shelly's exact illness is unknown, it's implied that modern medicine can't save her.
[
3
]
While not eager to die, Ms. Shelly was content with her life and has accepted her eventual death.
[
4
]
Bebop's motivation and wish in the ritual is to cure Shelly Fisher of her illness.
[
5
]
[
6
]
He views Ms. Shelly as his "mother".
[
7
]
Shelly doesn't know Bebop is a pit fighter, and he believes she wouldn't approve of his activities.
[
1
]
Shelly Fisher is resting at home during the in-game
ritual
.
[
8
]
Ms. Shelly's creation of Bebop is described as "an act of craftsmanship, willpower and sacrifice". Shelly created Bebop without any bindings (rules to keep Bebop under control) because she "doesn't believe in that sort of thing".
[
9
]
McGinnis
is impressed by the craftsmanship used to create Bebop and shows interest in hiring Ms. Shelly to work at
Fairfax Industries
.
[
10
]
Ms. Shelly is a fan of Jazz, which was the inspiration for the name Bebop.
[
11
]
Ms. Shelly's doctor is
Johann Geist Jr.
,
Lady Geist
's grandson.
[
12
]

[
edit
|
edit source
]

lore.title: Shopkeeper
lore.content:
The Shopkeeper in the Curiosity Shop in-game.
“
I don't know what that does, but my kid says all the mystics are buying it!
— The Shopkeeper
The
Shopkeeper
is a character in game who mans
The Curiosity Shop
stall and sells
items
to players.
The Shopkeeper has specific voice lines triggered by buying certain items, or for the player's hero when they buy a T4 item.
The Shopkeeper is voiced by Jason Keller,
[
1
]
who also voices the
Newscaster
.
Lore
[
edit
|
edit source
]
The Shopkeeper has a wife and kid, implying that he has a mortal body.
[
2
]
[
3
]
The Shopkeeper's kid is implied to be old enough to go to Columbia College.
[
4
]
The Shopkeeper is a big fan of the
New York Knickerbockers
basketball team and dislikes their rivals, the
Celtics
.
[
5
]
[
6
]
[
7
]
While a fan of the Knickerbockers, it's implied that the Shopkeeper broke the legs of a Knickerbocker player for owing him money.
[
8
]
[
9
]
The Shopkeeper plays in
Wraith
's weekly poker games, although he needs permission from his wife to play.
[
10
]
[
11
]
He describes himself as a "degenerate gambler".
[
12
]
Wraith also has a marker on him.
[
13
]
The Shopkeeper has been robbed by
Vyper
in the past.
[
14
]
Despite this, he still likes Vyper and considers her "his kind of scumbag".
[
15
]
The Shopkeeper dislikes Santa Claus.
[
16
]

lore.title: Soul Urn
lore.content:
“
You'd think people would be more excited about a jar of souls!
— Soul Urn
The
Soul Urn
, also known as
Spirit Urn, Soul Jar
or
Spirit Jar
, is an objective that spawns at the 10-minute mark and respawns in 5-minute intervals.
Players can pick up the urn and deliver it to its destination on the opposite side of the map. Doing so grants the delivering team
Soul Orbs
, a
Golden statue
buff, and additional souls for the courier.
Soul Urn icon
Carrying the soul urn
[
edit
|
edit source
]
After 10 minutes, the Soul Urn spawns in one of two locations in the outermost lanes: beside the overhanging bridge at York Street (Yellow Lane) or beside the bridge at Park Avenue (Green Lane). The urn always spawns first on Yellow Lane at 10 minutes and alternates lanes every 5 minutes. It descends from the sky for 12 seconds before it can be picked up. A player can grab the landed urn by standing near it for 2 seconds. Being
melee
'd or
stunned
while picking up the urn resets its channel time, and the player cannot
parry
while picking it up.
While carrying the urn, the courier is affected in several ways:
They get a
Move Speed
bonus of +3.5 m/s (capped at 15 m/s max), get their
Sprint Speed
disabled and have 100%
Slow Resistance
and 30%
Bullet
and
Spirit Resist
.
They are
disarmed
,
silenced
, and cannot use active items.
Channeled abilities and items started before picking up the urn, like
Ivy
's
Air Drop
and
Magic Carpet
, are not interrupted. Abilities can still be activated for about half a second while picking up urn.
Viscous
cannot carry as
Goo Ball
.
While Ivy can no longer pick up the urn while activating her ult, she can still fly allies who are carrying it, or pick the urn up during ult if she stays over it while flying. The latter can be achieved by dropping the urn inside a room with a low roof to fly inside.
They are revealed to the enemy team on the
Minimap
.
They cannot use
Ziplines
or
Teleporters
.
Going through
the Doorman
's
Doors
drops the urn at the entrance door.
Rem
and
Calico
(during
Ava
) cannot enter
vents
.
The courier can drop the urn at any time by pressing their
parry
key. A
melee attack
(light or heavy) or any
stun
from an enemy (including
Troopers
) will also force the courier to drop it. If the urn is left on the ground for too long, it will walk back to its spawn point.
Note:
The Soul Urn's soul value is determined at the moment it spawns (e.g., 10:00, 15:00). Holding onto the urn for longer durations does not increase its value.
Drop-off location
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Green candles indicating favored drop-off on the player's side
The drop-off point is on the opposite side of the map, marked by a Soul Urn outline icon on the Minimap and colored red if an enemy player is currently carrying the urn. The urn is instantly turned in when the courier steps onto the drop-off (being in the air doesn't count).
If a team is behind by a certain soul threshold (15% for the first urn, 10% after), the drop-off point moves closer to their side. This "favored" status is indicated by four floating green candles around the urn, and a green arrow over the urn pointing to the favored side in the minimap. For the leading team (drop-off on the enemy's side), the candles and minimap arrow are red.
The drop-off location is determined when an urn is first picked up by any player. Once determined, the drop-off point is fixed until the next urn spawn, even if the urn walks back to its spawn or the team soul difference changes.
Anti-stalling mechanics
[
edit
|
edit source
]
To prevent one team from holding the urn for too long without delivering it, the urn begins to damage its courier for 1.5% of their max health per second and disables their
Health Regen
after carrying it for 45 seconds. Furthermore, if the urn is dropped after that point the courier's entire team is prevented from picking up the urn again for 12 seconds. The 45-second timer pauses if the urn is dropped and resets if the enemy team picks it up.
Soul urn reward
[
edit
|
edit source
]
The Soul Urn's base soul bounty is
1300 Souls
and increases by
230 Souls per minute
of game time. This value is "locked in" at the minute the Urn spawns.
10-minute Urn:
3,600 Souls
20-minute Urn:
5,900 Souls
30-minute Urn:
8,200 Souls
When a team successfully turns in the Soul Urn:
A random
Golden Statue buff
is granted to each member of the capturing team.
50% of the base value is distributed
instantly and equally
to all team members.
50% of the base value is spawned as contestable
Soul Orbs
.
Courier's bonus
[
edit
|
edit source
]
The courier receives a personal bonus equal to
35% of their total share
(Base Share + Comeback Bonus). This bonus is awarded
instantly
upon delivery and cannot be denied by the enemy team. They also get 4 random
Golden Statue
buffs.
The comeback mechanic
[
edit
|
edit source
]
“If we are to complete the ritual, we must think clearly.”
This page may contain
factual inaccuracies
. The information presented requires verification against reliable sources.
Issue:
The math for comeback bounty may be inaccurate and needs more testing
If the capturing team is behind by
10% or more
(15% for the first urn), the Urn becomes a Comeback Urn. This adds a
Comeback Bonus
to the reward:
Bonus Calculation:
The bonus scales based on game time and the team's soul deficit. It uses a growth rate of
130 Souls per minute
.
Approximate Formula:
Individual Bonus = 130 × Minutes × Deficit %
Safe Payout:
The entire Comeback Bonus is added to the
Instant Soul
pool.
Orbs:
The contestable Soul Orbs
do not increase
during a comeback; they remain fixed at 50% of the original base share.
Note:
The first 3,000 souls of net worth difference are generally ignored when calculating the deficit, which may slightly dampen the bonus for smaller leads.
Reward scenarios
[
edit
|
edit source
]
The following examples use the
10-minute Soul Urn
(Base Value: 3,600 Souls).
Scenario 1: Standard delivery (Team is winning or even)
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Individual Base Share:
600 Souls (3,600 ÷ 6)
Orbs per player:
300 Souls (50% of share, deniable)
Instant per player:
300 Souls (50% of share, guaranteed)
Each Teammate receives:
600 Souls total (300 instant + 300 from orbs)
The Courier receives:
810 Souls total (300 instant + 300 orbs + 210 courier bonus)
The courier's 35% bonus is: 600 × 0.35 = 210 souls.
Scenario 2: Comeback delivery (30% deficit)
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Individual Base Share:
600 Souls
Comeback Bonus per player:
130 × 10 × 0.30 = 390 Souls
Total Individual Share:
990 Souls (600 base + 390 bonus)
Each Teammate receives:
990 Souls total (690 instant + 300 from orbs)
Instant souls include 300 (base) + 390 (comeback).
The Courier receives:
1,337 Souls total (1,037 instant + 300 orbs)
Courier bonus is: 990 × 0.35 = 346.5 souls.
Instant souls include 300 (base) + 390 (comeback) + 346.5 (bonus).
Note:
Team Instant
refers to the total non-deniable souls granted to the team (Instant shares + Courier bonus). All fractional soul values have been rounded.
Update history
[
edit
|
edit source
]
See all updates:
Soul Urn Update history
Lore
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Trapper
builds Soul Urns for the New York Municipal Coven.
[
1
]
The Urn is sentient and says its job is to keep New York clean by capturing rogue spirits, which should then be released in another location.
The Urn nags heroes to be delivered quickly and believes its work is underappreciated.
Trivia
[
edit
|
edit source
]
In
Neon Prime
, the Urn was called the
Idol
and dropped off at the Center Temple, a location above the
Mid-Boss
.
See also
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Soul Urn/Quotes
References
[
edit
|
edit source
]
↑
Trapper
's background -
"An employee of the New York Municipal Coven; Trapper spends his days building Spirit Urns, luring giant rats, and capturing various invasive species from The Outer Planes."

lore.content:
The Geist family tombstone

For the timeline of Deadlock's development, see
Deadlock#Development

“
Most people think the supernatural came into the world 50 years ago. They’re wrong.
—

The following is a list of known dated events in the
Deadlock
timeline.
Timeline
[
edit
|
edit source
]
The Deadlock timeline and time period are kept purposefully ambiguous, as the game is still in development and story aspects are subject to change. However, based on context clues such as references to the 1949 musical
South Pacific
[
1
]
[
2
]
[
3
]
[
4
]
by various characters, it can be inferred that the game takes place circa the 1930s-1940s (see
Historical References
below). The buildings in the Cursed Apple use architectural references from New York City up to 1932.
[
5
]
Even so, there are visual references from later in the 20th century:
Shiv
's design is reminiscent of a 1950s
Greaser
, while
Billy
is inspired by 1970s
Punks
. The computer in the
Hideout
is similar to the 1973
Xerox Alto
, one of the first personal computers, albeit with a typewriter keyboard.
The timeline below is based on mentioned in-game events and the
Lady Geist visual novel
. Events without a specific year use the date of the ritual as a reference point.

lore.title: Teams
lore.content:
“Let's get started, shall we?”
This article is a
stub
. You can help the Deadlock Wiki by expanding it.
In
Deadlock
, there are two opposing teams in the game, each consisting of 6 players. Each team serves either the
Archmother
or the
Hidden King
who seek to use the players to complete a ritual and fully realize their existence into the physical world.
In Standard,
Street Brawl
, and Bot matches, players cannot choose the team they fight for, as this is decided at the start of every match by the matchmaking engine. Between the two teams, each player will be playing a unique hero. On the contrary, Custom Matches will allow players to choose their own respective team and hero where they are not restricted to only having one of a specific hero in either team.
In-game
[
edit
|
edit source
]
During a game, a player works with their team to take down objectives and ultimately destroy the enemy Patron, while defending their own. Each team will be accompanied with a Guardian, a Walker, and a pair of Base Guardians for each lane.
Players can identify the team they are in from the voice of their Patron and the surrounding design of their team's side of the map.
The Archmother has a feminine voice, in which its base is topped with multiple Owl Statues and is named 'Grand Central'. Its team covers the 'Uptown' side of the map which is characterized by its mostly white buildings, and featuring an Aggressive Bull Statue beside the base to the right.
Meanwhile, The Hidden King has a masculine voice and its base is named Penn Station.  Its team covers the 'Downtown' side of the map which instead is characterized by its mostly red buildings, and its Defensive Bear and Horned Gargoyle statue beside on the right and on top the base, respectively.
During a game, the player will view their friendly Patron and Troopers to be in light-greenish blue, while the enemy Patron and Troopers are in red.
Heroes
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Main article:
Heroes
Before players can join a game, they are required to pick at least three heroes. When new heroes are introduced in a patch, players must include at least two non-new characters in their roster. A Standard and Bot Match will have 12 different unique heroes between the two teams while a Street Brawl will only have 8 unique heroes between the two teams. Custom Matches may involve multiple of the same heroes within or between the teams, or with lesser than 12 players in total.

lore.title: The Archmother
lore.content:
Overview
Quotes
The Archmother
“
Glass, steel and stone... Order and power where once there was none... The Archmother has always guided the founders' hands, and now it is time for them to welcome her home.
—
Old Gods, New Blood Update
The Archmother
is a
Patron
, as well as the name of her
Team
. Her followers are
The Archmother's Disciples
.
The Hidden King
is her rival.
The Archmother's herald is voiced by
Debra WIlson
.
[
1
]
Overview
[
edit
|
edit source
]
The Archmother wears a broken mask that resembles the head of the
Statue of Liberty
, her form bound to a crystalline effigy burning with blue flames. She's characterized by the color blue. Her base is a museum with a
Neoclassic
aesthetic. Her side of the
map
has upper-class white brick buildings and is labeled "Uptown".
The Archmother does not speak to the
heroes
directly. Instead, a herald speaks on her behalf.
[
2
]
[
3
]
Akin to the Hidden King, the Archmother's goals are unknown. According to
The Doorman
, the Archmother has wanted to be summoned for "centuries".
[
4
]
Warden
and his family believe the Patrons will "herald the end of humanity" if left unchecked.
[
5
]
It's implied that the Archmother wants to "usher in her new era".
[
6
]
[
7
]
The Doorman refers to the Archmother as
City Mother
.
[
8
]
The bank behind Archmother's Yellow
Walker
is called "City Mother Bank".
The Hidden King's herald calls the Archmother a "tyrant".
[
9
]
Gallery
[
edit
|
edit source
]
The Archmother
The Archmother (Weakened)
Archmother's base
Archmother artwork
Archmother typography
Archmother icon
Old Sapphire Flame Patron
"The Guided Hand" unused name
Unused Archmother icon
Unused Archmother Background
Trivia
[
edit
|
edit source
]
The Archmother was originally named
The Sapphire Flame
prior to the
Old Gods, New Blood
update. She also referred to herself as
Whisper in the Wind
and
Ambition's Spark
in an unused voice line.
[
10
]
This is referenced in a
Warden
unselect line.
[
11
]
A proposed rename to the Archmother was
The Guided Hand
.
[
12
]
References
[
edit
|
edit source
]
↑
2024-08-26 -
Kaori
;
Deadlock Discord
-
"[Sapphire Flame] is not Ellen [McLain], it's Debra Wilson"
↑
Archmother
's in-game
voice line
(Viscous start):
"Everyone looks at you and discounts your value, but not me, and not the Archmother. Complete the ritual, and let her help you."
↑
Archmother
's in-game
voice line
(ally Drifter killstreak):
"Archmother... accept the gift that the Drifter has laid before us!"
↑
The Doorman
's in-game
voice line
(Hotel Guest taunt):
"The Patrons have waited centuries to be summoned, they can wait a little longer."
↑
Warden
's in-game
voice line
(select):
"The Patrons will herald the end of humanity if we let them."
↑
Archmother
's in-game
voice line
(ally Graves killstreak):
"Graves will usher us into the Archmother's new era!"
↑
Archmother
's in-game
voice line
(Celeste start):
"Summon the Archmother, and usher in a new world with you by her side!"
↑
The Doorman
's in-game
voice line
(pre-game ping) -
"How exciting to see City Mother and the Amber King once more!"
↑
Hidden King
's in-game
voice line
(Venator start):
"Venator, I know you have no love for the Hidden King, but the Archmother is a tyrant! And together, we can usurp her!"
↑
Sapphire Flame
's
voice lines
↑
Warden
's in-game
voice line
-
"I won't act on behalf of Ambition Spark."
↑
Archmother
's in-game
voice line
(Doorman start):
"You honour the Guided Hand by standing with us today."

lore.title: The Arroyos
lore.content:
“
My life started when I met the Arroyos. Anything before that doesn't really matter to me.
—
Ivy
The Arroyos are
Ivy
's surrogate family that live in
Spanish Harlem
.
Summary
[
edit
|
edit source
]
20 years before the ritual takes place,
[
1
]
the Arroyos were a small family that lived in Spanish Harlem while it was under
Mendoza
's control. One day, Sophia Arroyo and one of her parents were attacked in the streets by thugs, but were saved by
Ivy
, who was then adopted by the Arroyos. For the next 18 years, Ivy spent her life fighting against the Mendoza Syndicate to free Spanish Harlem for the Arroyos.
[
2
]
In the current day, the Arroyos live happily with Ivy in a Mendoza-free Spanish Harlem. Carlos and Maria had a son named "Manny", while Sophia was heading off for college. Ivy thinks the Arroyos are the best family in the world.
[
3
]
Ivy is reluctant to leave the Arroyos on their own for too long.
[
4
]
[
5
]
This could be due to the fear that the Arroyos will be hurt if she's away from them.
[
6
]
[
7
]
[
8
]
The Arroyos eat
Mofongo
, a traditional Puerto-Rican dish.
[
2
]
[
9
]
The Arroyos are likely Catholic, based on Ivy's reaction to killing
Venator
.
[
10
]
[
11
]
There are four known Arroyos: Carlos, Maria, Sophia and Manny.
Carlos Arroyo
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Carlos Arroyo is the father of the family.
Carlos works as a first-grade teacher
[
12
]
and a community organiser for Spanish Harlem, however he's also running for mayor of New York.
[
13
]
In one pathway in
Ivy's visual novel
, Ivy supports Carlos' political campaign and participates in the ritual to help him become mayor.
[
14
]
Infernus
is voting for Carlos to become mayor.
[
15
]
Billy
thinks Carlos is a fraud who uses Ivy for publicity and to increase the property value of Spanish Harlem.
[
16
]
[
17
]
Carlos is worried about Ivy participating in the ritual.
[
18
]
He dislikes
Fairfax Industries
[
19
]
and isn't thrilled about Ivy fighting against the
OSIC
during the ritual.
[
20
]
Ivy idolises Carlos.
[
21
]
[
22
]
Carlos tells Ivy that it takes more muscles to frown than to smile.
[
23
]
He also says that "you can make opportunities, but you can't make people take them".
[
24
]
Maria Arroyo
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Maria Arroyo is the mother of the family.
Not much is known about Maria, however she does have a date planned with Carlos at the
Baroness Hotel
.
[
25
]
Ivy also doesn't want her to know about Venator's involvement in the ritual.
[
11
]
Sophia Arroyo
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Sophia Arroyo, also known as Sophie, is the elder daughter of the Arroyos. Based on her being a child when Ivy was brought to life, Sophia's age is likely in her mid-twenties.
Unknown to Ivy and the other Arroyos, Sophia works at
Wraith
's casino, helping Wraith as her bookmaker.
[
26
]
[
27
]
In one pathway in
Ivy's visual novel
, Ivy discovers Sophia's secret job.
[
28
]
While the ending of that pathway is unknown, it likely results in Ivy participating in the ritual to keep Sophia safe.
[
29
]
Sophia attends Columbia College as one of
Dynamo
's students.
[
30
]
Sophia speaks highly of Dynamo
[
31
]
[
32
]
and Dynamo thinks she's a great student, but he is worried about her due to Sophia sleeping during lessons due to working long shifts at her job.
[
33
]
Dynamo is unaware that Sophia works for Wraith.
Sophia tells Ivy that, if she gets mad, she should hold her breath and count to four.
[
34
]
Sophia was the Arroyo who named Ivy.
[
2
]
Sophia listens to
Cadence
's band.
[
35
]
Manny Arroyo
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Manny Arroyo is the younger son of the Arroyos. Manny's age is unknown, however he's implied to still be in school.
[
36
]
Manny seems to be a bookworm, as he loves reading about
Kelvin
's adventures
[
37
]
and gets taken to libraries by Ivy for
Paige
's Story Time.
[
38
]
Trivia
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Spanish Harlem
, also known as East Harlem or
El Barrio
, is a real New York neighborhood with majority Hispanic population.
The Arroyos seem to be spelt "The Arroyo's" instead of "The Arroyos", based on
Ivy
's background.
[
2
]
The Doorman
says that one of the Arroyos will die in 5 years, no matter what Ivy does.
[
7
]
This could be a bluff, but it's unknown.
References
[
edit
|
edit source
]
↑
Ivy's Visual Novel
-
Chapter 1
-
Ivy
:
"For the past 20 years I was so focused on cleaning up Spanish Harlem, I never really thought about what to do when I was finished."
↑
2.0
2.1

's background

Not that dismantling the Mendoza Syndicate isn't contributing, that's just more... contributing with a "capital C", as opposed to like, buying groceries."

lore.title: The Baxter Society
lore.content:
“
The Baxter Society has your number.
—
Shiv
The Baxter Society
is a well-known international monster hunting organization.
Summary
[
edit
|
edit source
]
The Baxter Society was formed sometime after the
First Maelstrom
, with
Grey Talon
as one of the founding members. The society has been operating for over 40 years, hunting monsters across the globe.
[
1
]
Currently,
Shiv
is the only named active Baxter Society member, with Grey Talon and
Calico
being former members.
Instead of hunting all
magical creatures
, the Baxter Society specifically hunts monsters that prey on humanity.
[
1
]
This can extend to hunting humans, as Grey Talon mentions that the society likely would have hunted
Lady Geist
if they knew about her deal with
Oathkeeper
.
[
2
]
It's also mentioned that, while they currently aren't hunting them, the Baxter Society would likely hunt down
Fathom
and
Seven
.
[
3
]
[
4
]
The Baxter Society have been hunting
Drifter
for years, but have been unsuccessful at killing him.
[
5
]
[
6
]
[
7
]
The members of the Baxter Society don't get paid to hunt monsters. The lack of payment was why Calico left the society to pursue the well-paid life of an assassin.
[
8
]
It's implied that Calico left the society before Shiv joined.
[
9
]
Grey Talon, while retired, is still heavily respected by the Baxter Society.
[
10
]
His dedication to the society resulted in books being made about his life and earning him a reputation as a "legend".
[
11
]
[
12
]
His dedication to his work, however, meant he was often away from home, which meant he missed watching his son grow up.
[
13
]
Refusing to make the same mistake for his grandkids, Grey Talon retired after roughly 40 years of service.
[
14
]
When he retired, Grey Talon was said to have more confirmed kills than anyone else in the Baxter Society.
[
15
]
Shiv was chosen by Grey Talon as his replacement.
[
16
]
[
17
]
Due to his criminal background, the other members of the Baxter Society don't trust or respect Shiv.
[
18
]
[
19
]
Shiv worries that the Baxter Society would eventually turn on him.
[
20
]
Shiv has been sent to the ritual on behalf of the Baxter Society,
[
21
]
[
22
]
and hopes that he can prove his worth to them and Grey Talon by hunting the
Patrons
.
[
23
]
[
24
]
Holliday
is disappointed that the Baxter Society isn't hunting
the Troubadour
. Shiv says the Society is dealing with their own problems, which could potentially be more dangerous than the Troubadour.
[
25
]
He also invites Holliday to join the Society if she manages to kill the Troubadour.
[
26
]
The Baxter Society owns a manor house, which seems to be their main base of operations. Once, a member of the
Paradox Organisation
tried to break into the manor house, but was caught.
Paradox
wants Grey Talon to get her into the manor house.
[
27
]
At some point, the Society had to intervene at the
Baroness Hotel
, which
The Doorman
was grateful for.
[
28
]
The Baxter Society have a friendly rivalry with the
Venators of St. Benedict
.
[
29
]
According to Grey Talon, members of the society who are killed in action are often found skinless in a retention pond in Portugal or Denmark.
[
30
]
Billy
believes the Baxter Society never hunted any real monsters.
[
31
]
Members
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Grey Talon
- Founding member, now retired
Shiv
- Member
Calico
- Former member
Trivia
[
edit
|
edit source
]
All 3 known members of the Baxter Society have a prosthetic right arm.
Originally, all the known members were described as parents.
[
32
]
[
33
]
A reference to Shiv being a father was removed in the
Old Gods, New Blood
update.
[
34
]
References
[
edit
|
edit source
]
↑
1.0
1.1
Grey Talon
's background:
"A founding member of "The Baxter Society", Wesley Grey Talon spent close to 40 years hunting monsters that preyed on man before finally deciding to find a replacement and lay down his bow."
↑
Grey Talon
And
Lady Geist Conversation 3
↑
Calico
's in-game
voice line
(killed Fathom) -
"If I didn't kill him, the Baxter Society would have eventually."
↑
Grey Talon
And
Seven Conversation 1
-
"If I was still in the Baxter Society, they would probably ask me to hunt you down."
↑
Grey Talon
's in-game
voice line
(killed Drifter):
"Finally..."
↑
Drifter
's in-game
voice line

lore.title: The Binding of Oathkeeper
lore.content:
“This is going to be exciting!”
This page contains game content that is unfinished, work in progress, or unavailable in standard gameplay.
Cover art
In-game menu
The Binding of Oathkeeper
is an upcoming
Deadlock
visual novel
starring
Lady Geist
.
The novel is accessible in-game through the
console command
citadel_book_open 1
. The novel is playable with audio, but the images have been removed and only the first chapter is unlocked. An earlier build with images has been archived on
YouTube.
See also:
The Binding of Oathkeeper/Transcription
Load video
YouTube
YouTube might collect personal data.
Privacy Policy
Continue
Dismiss
Full visual novel on YouTube
Characters
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Lady Geist
Dynamo
McGinnis
Infernus
Abrams
Bebop
Oathkeeper
Johann Geist (Jr.)
Johann Geist
Maximilian Fairfax
Ms. Shelly
Hank
Chapters
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Due to the branching nature of the visual novel, the chapters aren't listed here strictly in chronological order. For example, Chapter 1 leads to a choice between Chapter 2A and 2B, Chapter 2B leads to a choice between Chapter 3B and 3C, and so on. Youtuber
Dedlocko
has edited videos of the visual novel paths in the intended narrative order.
Load video
YouTube
YouTube might collect personal data.
Privacy Policy
Continue
Dismiss
Visual novel path "A" in intended order
Some of the endings aren't listed in the in-game visual novel UI, and as such it isn't clear if they will be available in the final version of the visual novel.
Chapter 1
[
edit
|
edit source
]
1 - The Oathkeeper Problem
Lady Geist discovers that the ward keeping a malevolent spirit known as
Oathkeeper
bound to her is weakening.
Johann
visits Geist at her apartment. Geist hides the blood of her most recent victim under the rug. She complains that Oathkeeper is growing more restless and hungry. Johann analyses her ward and concludes that it is weakening. Johann and Geist look for ways to build a new ward, or an alternative to control Oathkeeper.
Chapter 2
[
edit
|
edit source
]
2a - Party Planning
Geist meets with
Professor Dynamo
to host a symposium on wards.
2b - Understanding Oathkeeper
Geist meets with
Johann
to discuss Oathkeeper.
Chapter 3
[
edit
|
edit source
]
3a - The Gala
Geist has an encounter with someone who can help her.
3b - Hunger Strike
Geist threatens Oathkeeper in order to get him to agree to finding a new host.
3c - A New Deal
Geist dreams of the day she made her original deal with Oathkeeper.
Chapter 4
[
edit
|
edit source
]
4a - Humble Pie
Geist apologizes to
McGinnis
.
4b - Revelation
Geist meets with
Detective Abrams
.
4c - Decisions Decisions
Geist considers her options in pawning off Oathkeeper.
4d - Tea Time
Geist reveals to Johann the lengths she will go to in order to ensure her survival.
4e - Paying off the tab
Geist tries to work off 50 years of life that she hadn't paid Oathkeeper for.
Chapter 5
[
edit
|
edit source
]
5a - A New Ward
Geist's ward receives an upgrade courtesy of McGinnis.
5b - The Binding of Two
Geist and Abrams ask the Patrons to bind Oathkeeper to the Tome.
5c - Deal with the Devil
Geist tries to make
Maximilian Fairfax
an irresistible offer.
5d - The Last Temptation of Geist
Geist encounters
Ms. Shelly
. (Endings: Ignore the words of Shelly Fisher; Perpetuate the Cycle; Break the Cycle)
5g - The Sacrifice
Geist decides Johann's fate. (Endings: Sacrifice Him; Be Selfless)
5i - All Good Things
Geist makes peace with Oathkeeper and herself.

lore.title: The Cursed Apple
lore.content:
“
Welcome to New York! Need a gun?
— The
Shopkeeper
The Cursed Apple
or
"The Map"
is the default location where matches take place in
Deadlock
. It is a small segment of the game's version of Midtown and Lower Manhattan in New York City, in which the
Heroes
fight to complete
The Ritual
and summon their respective
Patron
. Players can navigate with a
Minimap
.
The current map is called
dl_midtown
. Previous versions of the map were called
street_test
and
dl_streets
.
Overview
[
edit
|
edit source
]
A Juke Room, with a shadow figure in its doorway.
Layout
[
edit
|
edit source
]
The map is divided into three distinct lanes:
York
(Yellow Lane),
Broadway
(Blue Lane) and
Park
(Green Lane), also referred to as
Greenwich
. Outside of these lanes are a series of tunnels, alleyways, and buildings that sometimes contain
Golden Statues
, crates, and neutral
Denizens
. In each lane, a
Zipline
extends from both bases directly overhead with each having the color of the corresponding lane.
The Hidden King's
side of the map has mostly worn down red brick buildings, while
the Archmother's
side has mostly upper class white brick buildings.
Throughout the map, players can find destructible Boxes and Golden Statues. Boxes have a 60% chance to drop
souls
upon being destroyed, and Golden Statues have a 50% chance to drop permanent stat bonuses, such as
Spirit Power
or
Weapon Damage
. They spawn 2 minutes into the game (except mid-boxes, see
Map Timings and Events : 10min
) and will respawn 3 minutes after being destroyed.
Outside of the lanes is an area of the map called the jungle, which is home to the neutral
Denizen
camps. Denizens are alien-like entities that appear in groups and will attack when provoked. They come in three different sizes: small, medium and large, and drop more souls the larger they are. Being neutral units, both teams can attack the same denizen camps, and they will be aggressive to their attackers no matter which team they are on.
Juke Rooms
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Juke Rooms are small dead-end tunnels located in specific points around the map. They are marked prominently by their shadowy figure standing in their doorways that contains a
cosmic veil
. Above the doorways is a red rectangular sign, and in the compartment contains candles and a small wall light. Juke Rooms - as implied by their name - are most often used to hide or corner enemies, as they have only one entrance. The doorway shadow also acts as a warning for fleeing players that they are about to walk into a dead-end.
A player, upon pinging a lane, has their hero state which lane they are headed to.
Objectives
[
edit
|
edit source
]
In each lane on either team's side, there are a series of powerful defensive structures that must be destroyed in order to reach the enemy base. The first is the
Guardian
, followed by the
Walker
and finally a pair of weaker
Base Guardians
. In each team's base are two
Shrines
, which must be destroyed in order to attack the main objective: the
Patron
. The Patron has two phases, and will shift to the second one after the first phase has been defeated. Destroying the now weakened Patron is the condition for winning a match.
In the very middle of the map, in the underground sewer, is a second major objective known as the
Mid-Boss
. It is a large alien creature similar to the Denizens, possessing a very large health pool, a constantly regenerating shield and a highly dangerous laser beam. After being slain, it will spawn a
Rejuvenator
Crystal that can be claimed by either team. The claimer's team will be bestowed with the Rejuvenation Credit buff, granting them special effects.
Player Visibility
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Main article:
Minimap
An important aspect of the map is spotting where enemies are located. An enemy player is visible to the team if they are either in a team player's line of sight, or shooting Troopers or Structures. A player can press Tab and middle-click the enemies' portrait to ping to their allies that said enemy is missing, which is useful if they have left their lane.
Map Icons
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Special locations on the map can be identified by the following icons:
* Mid-Boss respawn time decreases after each death, with a minimum time of 5 minutes.
Map Timings and Events
[
edit
|
edit source
]
0 -> 2 -> 5 ->  6 -> 8 -> 10 Minutes
The map at 0 minutes.
The map at 2 minutes.
2:00
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Easy
Denizen
camps
Respawn Time:
1:25 minutes after all Denizens in camp are defeated.
Count:
4
Crates
Respawn Time:
3 minutes after being broken.
Drops:
Unsecured Souls
(50-100 Souls on average, scales with game time).
Usage:
Unsecured Souls may be spent in the shop, but are spent
after
Secured Souls.
If the player die with Unsecured Souls, they appear on the ground where they died. Picking up the Souls grants the Unsecured Souls.
Denizens also drop Unsecured Souls.
Golden Statues
(Tier 1)
Respawn Time:
3 minutes after being broken.
Buffs Granted:
+1.5% Fire Rate
+3% Ammo Capacity
+0.75% Cooldown Reduction
+3% Weapon Damage
+15 Health
+2 Spirit
Note:
The boxes and statues in the room above
Mid-Boss
only spawn with Mid-Boss at 10:00.
The map at 5 minutes.
The map at 6 minutes.
5:00
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Powerups
Spawn and Respawn:
Every 5 minutes thereafter.
Example:
If picked up at 14:55, a new powerup will appear at 15:00.
Note:
Powerups spawn at both locations simultaneously, and their status can be checked via the mini-map icon.
6:00
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Medium
Denizen
Camps
Respawn Time:
4:50 minutes after all Denizens in camp are defeated.
Count:
22
The map at 8 minutes.
8:00
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Hard
Denizen
Camps
Respawn Time:
5:35 minutes after all Denizens in camp are defeated.
Count:
12
Vault Camp
(
Sinner's Sacrifice
machines)
Machine Effect:
Has 500 health and can only be damaged with melee attacks. Weak melee attacks will do 50 point of damage and strong melee attacks will do 100 (Weak melee will do 100 damage for last hit). Each hit will deal 80 points of damage to the attacker, but grant some amount of Souls. Once the machine takes 400 damage, a looping timing window starts where a well timed heavy melee attack will dispense four Golden Statue buffs. It will dispense one buff if killed with a light melee attack.
Respawn Time:
5 minutes after being defeated.
Count:
10 locations (some locations have 2 machines).
Troopers
Post-Lane Soul Mechanics:
The initial kill on a Trooper no longer grants Souls. Instead, all Souls are contained in the
Soul Orb
.
The map at 10 minutes.
10:00
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Soul Urn
Descent:
Begins at 10:00 and rotates to the opposite side of the map.
Landing Time:
12.5 seconds after it begins descending.
Respawn Time:
Every 5 minutes after 10:00.
Mid-Boss
Buff Granted:
4-minute
Rejuvenator
buff for all allies.
Effects:
Grants 1.7/2/2.3x (kill 1/2/3+) additional health to Troopers.
Reduces respawn time by 0.5/0.4/0.3x (kill 1/2/3+) for the next death.
Grants 20% additional Fire Rate to Heroes.
Grants 200 additional Health to Heroes.
Grants 1m/s additional Move Speed to Heroes.
Respawn Time:
7/6/5 minutes* after being defeated, decreasing with each respawn.
Mid Crates and Golden Statues
(in the room above Mid-Boss)
Golden Statues Upgraded (Tier 2)
Buffs Granted:
+2% Fire Rate
+5% Ammo Capacity
+1% Cooldown Reduction
+4% Weapon Damage
+25 Health
+3 Spirit
30:00
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Golden Statues Upgraded (Tier 3)
Buffs Granted:
+2.5% Fire Rate
+7% Ammo Capacity
+1.5% Cooldown Reduction
+6% Weapon Damage
+35 Health
+4 Spirit
Territory
[
edit
|
edit source
]
An important part of
Deadlock
is managing the clash of
Troopers
that occurs in each lane. Killing enemy Troopers allows allied Troopers to move further into the lane. This extends the
Zipline
available to the player's team on that lane. The Ziplines on the map will show how far a lane is "pushed" towards the enemy base, how far the Zipline can take the player, and if any lanes are in need of help with a quick glance.
Teleporters
can be used to quickly traverse the map horizontally.
Trivia
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Before the
Map Rework
Update, the map had 4 lanes. The fourth was Purple (Park). Green Lane was initially called Greenwich, then replaced Park, and is now Greenwich again, though still labeled as Park in the Zipline UI.  The center of the map was the Museum, now reworked as the Archmother base.
Green (Greenwich) originally supplanted Orange (Orchard).
All the lanes are named after real New York streets. The team's bases used to be named after the train stations Grand Central (Archmother) and Penn Station (Hidden King).
Towards the right of the team's bases is a Bull and a Bear Statue, respectively located at the Museum (Archmother) and the Factory (Hidden King). These statues are symbolic to the
market conditions
of a stock market. On a related note, the
Bull Statue
has a real world counterpart in New York.
The
Statue of Liberty
can be seen behind the dock on Yellow Lane.
The map used to be called
street_test
, and was later changed to
dl_streets
in one of the updates, until it was replaced by
dl_midtown
.
street_test
and
dl_streets
can still be accessed in-game with the
console command
map street_test
and
map dl_streets
, although the former's lighting was broken by updates.
There used to be a map in the files called
caldera_trooper,
which featured assets from both
Neon Prime
and early
Deadlock
builds. It has since been removed, but was archived by modders. A tour and comparison of
caldera_trooper
was made by YouTube channel
DeadAir
. Left side of the Sandbox map was based off it.
Other unused maps currently accessible in the game files that use
Neon Prime
assets are called
start
,
1v1_test
and
hero_testing
.
Many of the buildings in the Cursed Apple's skyline are based on real New York buildings from the early 1900s.
[
1
]
The Juke Room shadows are actually 3D models rendered with transparency. There are 4 models: A tall man wearing a fedora, a short man wearing a postman hat, a woman, and a child holding a box.
Gallery

edit

]

Neon Prime

]

]

lore.title: The Deep
lore.content:
“
I was hoping that this suit would make people take the problems of the Deep seriously. I'm not sure it's working.
—
Viscous
The Deep
is an underwater location within the depths of the
Bermuda Triangle
. It is connected to
Outer Planes
via an
Astral Gate
.
Summary
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Described as a shadowline nexus deep below the Atlantic Ocean, the Deep is an underwater habitat where an Astral Gate resides.
[
1
]
As the Deep is deep underwater, the surface world doesn't interact with it much, making its culture significantly different from the surface.
[
2
]
Viscous
and
Fathom
are denizens of the Deep.
[
1
]
[
3
]
The
Loch Ness Monster
also came from the Deep's Astral Gate.
[
1
]
The Deep is being threatened by a "genocidal force of nature" known as The Adversary, who came through the Deep's Astral Gate. The denizens of the Deep are terrified of the Adversary, as they are currently getting "massacred" by the monster.
[
4
]
Before the Adversary came along, Fathom was the apex predator of the Deep.
[
3
]
Some denizens of the Deep used to worship Fathom, begging him to save their lives from The Adversary.
[
5
]
The denizens of the Deep know Fathom's "true name".
[
6
]
Viscous says the other denizens find legs "unnerving", but he enjoys having them.
[
7
]
Calico
has knowledge about the Deep.
[
8
]
[
9
]
The
OSIC
seems to worry about the creatures of the Deep.
[
10
]
The Adversary
[
edit
|
edit source
]
The Adversary is a powerful and dangerous being who came through the Deep's Astral Gate. Currently, the Adversary is massacring the denizens of the Deep, and Viscous believes that, if he can't stop the Adversary, it's only a matter of time before the Adversary starts killing everything on the surface.
[
11
]
[
12
]
Both Viscous and Fathom have entered the ritual to stop the Adversary. Viscous wants to get rid of the Adversary to save the Deep, and Fathom wants to become the Apex Predator of the Deep.
[
1
]
[
3
]
Most playable characters are unaware of what's happening in the Deep, and don't take the threat of the Adversary seriously.
[
13
]
Billy
refuses to believe in the Adversary unless he sees it with his own eyes.
[
14
]
It's implied that the surface often ignores the troubles of the Deep.
[
15
]
[
16
]
Viscous asks
Grey Talon
if the Baxter Society could help stop the Adversary if he fails to summon the
Patrons
.
[
17
]
Haze
is aware of the Adversary and takes the situation seriously as she knows it's only a matter of time until the Adversary becomes a problem on the surface.
[
18
]
She asks Viscous to tell her everything about the Adversary so the OSIC can be prepared in case more creatures like it come through the Deep's Astral Gate.
[
19
]
Trivia
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Holliday
mistakes the Deep for the American Deep South. She asks Viscous if he's from
Mobile, Alabama
.
[
20
]
References
[
edit
|
edit source
]
↑
1.0
1.1
1.2
1.3
Viscous
' background
↑
Viscous
' in-game
voice line
(post-game ping):
"You're all welcome in the Deep, but you'd probably drown."
↑

's background

↑

"The sailors called me Fathom, but the denizens of the Deep know me by my true name."

' in-game

"Most of my kind find legs to be unnerving, but I rather enjoy them."

Calico
's in-game

"Ugh, creatures of the Deep."
↑
Calico
's in-game
voice line
(killed Viscous) -
"The Deep should have chosen a more competent emissary."
↑
Haze
's in-game
voice line
(killed Viscous):
"To think the OSIC was worried about your kind."
