lore.title: The Hidden King
lore.content:
Overview
Quotes
The Hidden King
“
The stretching shadow of a skyscraper... a boarded-up apartment in Hell’s Kitchen... The Hidden King exists in the cracks of the city, waiting for his followers to usher him into the light.
—
Old Gods, New Blood Update
The Hidden King
is a
Patron
, as well as the name of his
Team
. His followers are
The Hidden King's Court
.
The Archmother
is his rival.
Overview
[
edit
|
edit source
]
The Hidden King appears as a shadowy figure with antlers and glowing yellow eyes, bound to a burning
wicker man
effigy. Long, claw-like yellow hands extend from the shadows. His base is a factory filled with large speakers and microphones, and his side of the
map
features working-class red brick buildings labeled "Downtown".
The Hidden King does not speak to the
heroes
directly. Instead, a herald speaks on his behalf.
[
1
]
[
2
]
[
3
]
Akin to the Archmother, the Hidden King's goals are unknown. According to
The Doorman
, the Hidden King has wanted to be summoned for "centuries".
[
4
]
Warden
and his family believe the Patrons will "herald the end of humanity" if left unchecked.
[
5
]
It's implied that the Hidden King wants to "usher in his new era".
[
6
]
[
7
]
The Hidden King's herald mentions that the Hidden King is "returning to us".
[
2
]
The Doorman refers to the Hidden King as
Amber King
.
[
8
]
Gallery
[
edit
|
edit source
]
The Hidden King
The Hidden King (Weakened)
Hidden King's base
Hidden King artwork
Hidden King icon
Hidden King typography
Weakened Hidden King icon
Old Amber Hand Patron
"The Broken Crown" unused name
Unused Hidden King icon
Trivia
[
edit
|
edit source
]
The Hidden King's name and appearance have similarities to
The King in Yellow
, a fictional supernatural entity created by
Robert W. Chambers
.
The Hidden King was originally named
The Amber Hand
prior to the
Old Gods, New Blood
update. He also referred to himself as
Anvil's Song
in a removed voice line. This is referenced in a
Warden
unselect line.
[
9
]
A proposed rename to the Hidden King was
The Broken Crown
.
The Hidden King announcer's voice actor was recast with the new model in the
Old Gods, New Blood
update. The current voice is speculated to be either Clancy Brown (Mr. Krabs in
Spongebob Squarepants
) or Darin De Paul (Reinhardt in
Overwatch
). Developer
Kaori
has so far chosen not to reveal the name of either the current or previous voice actor to not "spoil the fun".
[
10
]
They have also said that the previous Amber Hand actor can still be heard in the game in a different role.
[
11
]
References
[
edit
|
edit source
]
↑
Hidden King
's in-game
voice line
(Unused tutorial line) -
"I speak for the Hidden King, and today I shall be your guide."
↑
2.0
2.1
Hidden King
's in-game
voice line
(McGinnis start):
"The Hidden King is honored by your presence, McGinnis. May your inventions pave the way for his return to us.
↑
Hidden King
's in-game
voice line
(Haze start):
"You are driven by duty, Haze. I know you won't fail the Hidden King."
↑
The Doorman
's in-game
voice line
(Hotel Guest taunt):
"The Patrons have waited centuries to be summoned, they can wait a little longer."
↑
Warden
's in-game
voice line
(select):
"The Patrons will herald the end of humanity if we let them."
↑
Hidden King
's in-game
voice line
(ally Graves killstreak):
"Graves will usher us into the Hidden King's new era!"
↑
Hidden King
's in-game
voice line
(Victor start):
"You need not feel adrift in this world, Victor. Complete the ritual, and find your place alongside the Hidden King!"
↑
The Doorman
's in-game
voice line
(pre-game ping) -
"How exciting to see City Mother and the Amber King once more!"
↑
Warden
's in-game
voice line
(unselect) -
"I won't be Anvil's Song's cat's paw."
↑
2026-01-26 -
@nookem
and
Kaori
; Deadlock Discord - nookem:
"kaori before you go can you answer if The Hidden King is darin de paul?"
Kaori:
"i mean i could, but why spoil the fun?"
↑
2026-01-23 -
Kaori
; Deadlock Discord -
"The actor who played the voice of the OG amber hand is still heard in the game"

lore.title: The Library
lore.content:
“This is going to be exciting!”
This page contains game content that is unfinished, work in progress, or unavailable in standard gameplay.
The Library menu.
The Library
is an upcoming
Deadlock
feature. The Library will contain visual novels that will expand the world and lore of
Deadlock
through interactive, branching narratives.
It is expected that cosmetic items may be unlocked as rewards for completing the visual novel paths.
The Library used to be accessible in-game in an earlier build, but has since been removed.
Summary
[
edit
|
edit source
]
The Binding of Oathkeeper
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Main article:
The Binding of Oathkeeper
Lady Geist
has to find a way to deal with
Oathkeeper
.
Infernus' visual novel
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Infernus
has to save the bar he works at,
Jezebel's
, from being closed.
Ivy's visual novel
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Main article:
Ivy's visual novel
Ivy
wants to get a job to help
the Arroyos
.
Holliday's visual novel
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Main article:
Holliday's visual novel
Holliday
arrives in
New York
on the hunt for
the Troubadour
.

lore.title: The Mendoza Syndicate
lore.content:
“
I took out Mendoza, and I'll take out you!
—
Ivy
(to
Yamato
)
The Mendoza Syndicate
was a crime syndicate located in Spanish Harlem, named after its leader
Mendoza
.
Summary
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Around
1929
, the Mendoza Syndicate controlled Spanish Harlem. Members of the Syndicate harassed innocent people, including the
Arroyo family
. At this time, the gargoyle that would become known as
Ivy
came to life and protected the Arroyos. Ivy was named and adopted by the family and vowed to dismantle the Mendoza Syndicate and free Spanish Harlem. She dedicated 18 years of her life to this mission.
[
1
]
Ivy tells
Vindicta
that she was able to take down the Syndicate "without murdering everybody."
[
2
]
Yamato
thanks Ivy for taking care of Mendoza.
[
3
]
However, she also undermines Ivy's skill and says she wants to take over Spanish Harlem for the Seventh Moon.
[
4
]
[
5
]
Wraith
admires Ivy for "making Mendoza's life hell." Ivy is wary of Wraith and warns her to stay away from Spanish Harlem.
[
2
]
Mendoza
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Mendoza is described as a "good client" by
Krill
, and a "bad man" by Ivy.
[
2
]
He told Wraith that
Lash
would take a dive in a fight, which she lost money betting on. Lash says Mendoza "says a lot of stupid shit."
[
6
]
The
leaked audio
for
Ivy's visual novel
implies that Mendoza promised to leave New York, but instead went into hiding. Ivy and Lash will be going after him.
References
[
edit
|
edit source
]
↑
Ivy
's background
↑
2.0
2.1
2.2
Ivy conversations
↑
Yamato
's in-game
voice line
(translated) -
"Thanks for taking care of Mendoza."
↑
Yamato
's in-game
voice line
(translated) -
"If someone like that can stop Mendoza, conquering New York won't be difficult."
↑
Yamato
's in-game
voice line
(translated) -
"Next, I'll take over Spanish Harlem. In return for your rudeness."
↑
Lash and Wraith conversation

lore.title: The New York Oracle
lore.content:
The New York Oracle billboard
The New York Oracle Newscaster
“
The New York Oracle, bringing the future to the present for over 20 years.
— The Newscaster
The
New York Oracle
is an in-universe newspaper publication in
Deadlock
that has been operating in New York for over 20 years, costing 5 pennies per copy.
In-game, it takes the form of a stall on the lanes with the Newscaster NPC. Although it can't be interacted with, it will periodically say one of the headlines, which give insights to nearby players about the world and setting of the game. The Newscaster model is similar to the
Shopkeeper
. The Newscaster also narrates the in-game tutorial.
Allie Kincaid
is a reporter for the New York Oracle.
The Newscaster is voiced by Jason Keller, who also voices the Shopkeeper.
[
1
]
Locations
[
edit
|
edit source
]
The Newscaster can be found in 2 locations across the
Cursed Apple
.
Close to the
Hidden King
's base in the Yellow lane
Close to the
Archmother
's base in the Green lane
Headlines
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Seasonal (Winter)
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Seasonal headlines are played during the winter
seasonal event
.
Hero Headlines
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Seasonal hero headlines are played when the player completes a winter skin challenge.
Trivia
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Most newspapers in the game currently use a placeholder texture from
Half-Life: Alyx
. The New York Oracle newsstands also use public domain images of real 20th Century newspapers.
↑
Deadlock
on
IMDb

lore.title: The Troubadour
lore.content:
“
When I find who I'm looking for, I'm not bringing him in. I'm putting him down.
—
Holliday
"The Troubadour"
is the name given by media for a serial killer who commited a killing spree in the town of
Macomb
in central Illinois.
Overview
[
edit
|
edit source
]
The Troubadour was confronted by the town's sheriff,
Holliday
, but he managed to escape by turning into a murder of crows and flying away, although Holliday still has been looking for him. Both she and the
New York Oracle
speculate that The Troubadour is flying from
Champaign–Urbana
to
New York
.
[
1
]
Holliday's visual novel
tells the story of her hunt for the Troubadour.
It is implied that Holliday's late sister might have been a victim of the Troubadour.
[
2
]
[
3
]
The Drifter
tells
Infernus
that he's not the Troubadour, and that he only knows him by reputation.
[
4
]
He claims to be more dangerous than the Troubadour,
[
5
]
and advises Holliday against hunting the Troubadour.
[
6
]
Holliday is disappointed that
the Baxter Society
isn't hunting the Troubadour.
Shiv
says the Society is dealing with their own problems, which could potentially be more dangerous than the Troubadour.
[
7
]
Calico
says she knows enough about the Troubadour to stay away from him.
[
8
]
One of Holliday's post-visual novel interaction with
Seven
suggests that the
OSIC
wants to recruit the Troubadour.
[
9
]
According to
The Doorman
, the Troubadour's actions in Macomb took away
the Baroness
' title of "most haunted hotel in America".
[
10
]
References
[
edit
|
edit source
]
↑
Newscaster
's in-game
voice line
-
"A murder of crows suspected to be the infamous serial killer known as 'The Troubadour' was spotted flying over Champaign-Urbana today. Could he be flying to New York?"
↑
Holliday
And
Ivy Conversation 1
-
"My sister's dead."
↑
Holliday
And
Grey Talon Conversation 3
-
"I know that look in your eyes. Who did they take from you? (...) I hope today we both find peace."
↑
Drifter
And
Infernus Conversation 2
-
"Level with me. Are you the Troubadour?"
-
"While I do have a song in my heart, I'm not. (...) Never met the man, just know him by reputation."
↑
Drifter
's in-game
voice line
-
"The Troubadour ain’t got nothin’ on me."
↑
Drifter
And
Holliday Conversation 1
-
"You don't wanna find him. (...)  Macomb matters a lot to you, but to him it was just a fun time that'll get lost in the ocean of memory. But if you find him, if he knows he got his hooks in you, you gonna be his hobby."
↑
Holliday
And
Shiv Conversation 2
-
"We got our own problems right now. (...) Trust me Sheriff, if we don't clean up our own house you'll be wishing for Troubadour-sized problems."
↑
Holliday
And
Calico Conversation 2
-
"You ever hear of something called 'The Troubadour'?"
-
"Enough to know to stay away from it."
↑
Holliday
And
Seven Conversation 3 (post-visual novel)
-
"I can't believe the OSIC wanted to recruit that thing."
↑
The Doorman
And
Grey Talon Conversation 3
-
"The Baroness still the most haunted hotel in America?"
-
"Just the tri-state area, I’m afraid. That ghastly business in Macomb pulled a lot of mid-west specters."

lore.title: The Tunnel Rats
lore.content:
“
You're going to wish you had the Tunnel Rats on your side.
—
Krill
The Tunnel Rats are an underground (both figuratively and literally) group that assists the black market that operates between the "boroughs", using a network of underground tunnels.
[
1
]
The Tunnel Rats used to do business with the
Mendoza Syndicate
.
[
2
]
Notable members
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Mo & Krill
act as enforcers for The Tunnel Rats, punishing anyone who disrespects the group. They oppose the gangs who occasionally try and exploit the tunnel system.
Trivia
[
edit
|
edit source
]
The description of the Tunnel Rats is mostly reused from the original iteration in
Neon Prime
lore
.
References
[
edit
|
edit source
]
↑
Mo & Krill
's background -
"The Tunnel Rats, a group that help facilitate illicit trade and commerce between the boroughs thanks to a network of underground tunnels."
↑
Krill and Ivy conversation

lore.title: United States of America
lore.content:
“
Next week will mark the 5 year anniversary of South Ixia officially joining the United States as its 52nd state.
— The
Newscaster
The United States of America
is a country on Earth in which the
Cursed Apple
is located.
Description
[
edit
|
edit source
]
The United States is composed of 52 states. The 50th State is the Island of Cadenza,
[
1
]
the 51st is Puerto Rico,
[
2
]
and the 52nd is
South Ixia
.
[
3
]
Other known states include New York, Wyoming,
[
4
]
Massachusetts,
[
5
]
[
6
]
Illinois,
[
7
]
Pennsylvania
[
8
]
and Wisconsin.
[
9
]
There are at least 2 known
Astral Gates
located in US territory: New York and Puerto Rico.
The
OSIC
is an organization within the US government.
The US government is sending aid to
Ixia
as part of their military campaign, drawing opposition from the
Friends of Humanity
.
[
10
]
The US government is negotiating a sale of a part of Wyoming with the
Djinn
.
[
4
]
[
11
]
Trivia
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Due to the divergences in the
Deadlock
timeline
, it is currently unknown if Hawaii and Alaska are US states, as they joined the Union in the year 1959 in the real world.
References
[
edit
|
edit source
]
↑
Newscaster
's in-game
voice line
-
"The Supreme Court is poised to rule on the Fairfax Fair Tax bill next session. Will our 50th state remain in the union? Or will Fairfax Industries successfully regain its sovereignty over the island of Cadenza? Only time will tell!"
↑
2024-08-31 -
Yoshi
, Deadlock Discord - "
US has 52 states, 51st is Puerto Rico which gained statehood after one of the gates to outer planes was discovered there. The 52nd is South Ixia (outer plane of Ixia where Infernus' parents immigrated from)"
↑
Newscaster
's in-game
voice line
-
"Next week will mark the 5 year anniversary of South Ixia officially joining the United States as its 52nd state. See how folks are planning to celebrate across the country in today's edition of The New York Oracle."
↑
4.0
4.1
Mirage
's background -
"It's the hope of the Djinn that they can negotiate with the US government for a sale of part of Wyoming so that they can build a sovereign nation for their people."
↑
Vindicta
's background -
"A victim of John Hathorne during the Salem Witch Trials"
↑
Shopkeeper
's in-game
voice line
-
"Wow, if it isn't the poster boy for the Celtics!"
↑
Holliday
's background -
"That was 6 months ago, and while the murders in central Illinois have stopped, Holliday's investigation has not."
↑
Vyper
's in-game
voice line
-
"You know, they love this in Philly."
↑
Vyper and Calico conversation
- Vyper:
"No, not New Jersey, Wisconsin! There's actually a lot of assassins in Wisconsin."
↑
Newscaster
's in-game
voice line
-
"As the military campaign in Ixia continues, the controversial political organization Friends of Humanity announced a rally in New York to demand the US cease sending aid to the Outer Planes."
↑
Newscaster
's in-game
voice line
-
"Djinn ambassador Nashala Dion has arrived in New York as negotiations continue. But is the US Government really okay with selling Wyoming?"

guides.title: Abilities
guides.content:
An ability is a unique skill of a hero that does damage, grants utility, or both. All heroes have 4 base abilities that are unlocked throughout the early stages of the game using ability unlocks, and upgraded via ability points. Abilities can be either active, passive, or partially passive, and will define how a hero plays, scales, builds and wins using a specific strategy.
Ability unlocks
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Abilities are unlocked with
Ability Unlocks
earned at
600
,
1200
,
2100
and
3600
soul thresholds (
Boon
levels 0, 2, 4 and 6). The first 3 non-ultimate abilities can be unlocked in any order, while the ultimate ability can only be unlocked at level 6. Ability unlocks cannot be refunded.
Ability points
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Infernus
Napalm
showing the 3 levels of upgrades an ability can have, at 1, 2 and 5 points. (Upgrades may be outdated)
Ability Points
are earned at every
boon
that does not give an ability unlock. These are used to upgrade the power of unlocked abilities, granting additional bonuses and better stats. All abilities have 3 tiers for upgrades, costing 1, 2, and 5 points to unlock. Unspent points are shown under the player's ability icons.
The player can refund an upgrade by clicking its slot up to 10 seconds after unlocking it, if they haven't used any abilities in that time window.
All 32 ability points can be earned by leveling up. Additionally, the player can earn 1 point earlier by delivering the
Soul Urn
.
Types of abilities
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Abilities come in three main categories:
active
,
passive
, and
partially passive
.
Active
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Active abilities are abilities that are used by pressing the input key, and have a cooldown before they can be used again. An active ability is linked to a hero's power and when it is on cooldown gives a window of opportunity for counterplay (see
strategy
for more). Active abilities cannot be used if the player is under the effects of a
stun
,
silence
or
displacement
. Active abilities can have a range of properties including being instant, channeled or having charges.
An
instant
ability is an ability that can be used only once immediately before being put on cooldown. It is seen as the most basic and straightforward type of abilities. An example of an instant active ability is
Bebop
's
Sticky Bomb
. Some active abilities can be recast during a specific timeframe, such as
Mina
's
Sanguine Retreat
, but will still go on cooldown after the first use while the recast timer is active.
A
channeled
ability is an ability that requires channeling it for a duration, indicated by a filled bar slowly diminishing on the characters HUD. The channel can be stopped before the channel duration is complete, and will go on cooldown after it's first press before the channel duration has completed. Channeled abilities often prohibit the player from
movement
but this is not a rule. F. ex.
Kelvin
's
Arctic Beam
allows walking around though at a slowed movement speed and crouching, but not
dashing
and
jumping
(the jumping key is default the key to cancel channeling).
Seven
's
Storm Cloud
prohibits all movement, unless upgraded to level 3. Channeled abilities can be stopped preemptively by and opponent by using a
stun
, but a
silence
or
displacement
will not stop an ongoing channeled ability. This does not affect the cooldown of the ability.
An ability with charges, showing 3 ready charges on the left and 4 ready charges on the right.
A
charged
ability - or, an ability with
charges
- is an ability that can be used multiple times in a row, and has a separate cooldown for the time between charges and the time for a charge to be restored. This is indicated by a white bar over the ability icon on the players HUD, and is split by a thin black line for the amount of charges the ability currently possess. A charge that is currently on cooldown is indicated by a gray bar slowly filling up in place of the white bar. Some abilities will also start as instant active, and gain charges with levels. For more info, please see the table of charged abilities:
* The ability needs to be upgraded to T3 before it is considered a charged ability
** The ability needs to be upgraded to T2 before it is considered a charged ability
Passive
[
edit
|
edit source
]
A passive ability is an ability that is not directly activatable by the player, but by some condition of the game. Passive abilities can not be turned off or deactivated in any way after they have been unlocked. A passive ability can affect either the hero itself, such as
Abrams
's
Infernal Resilience
or an enemy hero such as
Infernus
's
Afterburn
, and heroes with a passive ability will often have this ability as a defining trait signifying how they are played and built.
The passive abilities in Deadlock are:
Partial Passive
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Partial passive abilities are active abilities with passive components or a passive ability with an active trigger (and are therefore affected by silences, stuns, etc). These are:
Billy
's
Blasted
which has a passive component of reloading ammo and marking enemies, but can be activated. The passive effect persists through the active cooldown.
Mirage
's
Djinn's mark
which can be activated early, but the mark will build and trigger even if silenced.
Shiv
's
Bloodletting
has both deferring damage as a passive, and an active to clear said deferred damage.
Victor
's
Pain Battery
where the damage storing is handled as a passive, but the actual usage of the ability behaves as an active ability.
Ability Scaling and damage
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Abilities have three ways of being useful: dealing
spirit damage
, dealing
melee damage
or providing utility.
Spirit Damage
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Spirit damage is the main form of ability damage there is in
Deadlock
, and the vast majority of abilities will deal all or some of its damage as
spirit damage. This damage is generally split into two parts: the
base damage
represented by an integer, and the
spirit scaling
damage
represented by a percentage of the heros
spirit power
. The base damage is a base value that can only be changed via leveling up the ability using
Ability Points
, the spririt scaling damage can be increased by obtaining more
spirit power via
items
,
golden statues
,
powerups
or the
rejuvenator
. Please see the page on
spirit power scaling
for more details.
Melee Damage
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Melee damage
means that the ability will deal a percentage of the hero's current
melee damage on contact. This will also apply any effect currently attributed to the melee of the hero, such as
Spirit Strike
or
Melee Lifesteal
. Conversely, this also means the damage is reduced by a hero's
melee damage resist
from items like
Close Quarters
. Some heroes can upgrade their abilities to instead apply heavy
melee damage, and this will apply item effects that benefit from this such as
Melee Charge
. This will not, however, work with mechanics needing heavy melee attacks such as a
sinner's sacrifice
,
powerup
or a
rejuvenator
.
Abilities dealing
melee damage:
Utility
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Some abilities will have no damage dealt, but will instead provide the hero with some sort of utility to use while playing. This is a wide topic and can be anything from
Calico
's
Ava
providing mobility and eventually
health regeneration
,
Mo & Krill
's
Sand Blast
providing a
disarm status effect on enemies, and
Paige
's
Plot Armor
providing a barrier and weapon damage.
Scaling
[
edit
|
edit source
]
While most abilities scale on
spirit power
, there are some that have alternate ways of scaling. These are
McGinnis
's
Mini Turret
whose health scales on her own maximum health, and
Paradox
's
Kinetic Carbine
which scales on her
weapon damage
. These are the only two abilities that scale on something other than spirit power.
A few abilities have no scaling at all, only increasing in power when spending
Ability Points
to upgrade them. This includes
Lash
's
Grapple
,
Mo & Krill
's
Sand Blast
and
Grey Talon
's
Rain of Arrows
&
Spirit Snare
to name a few.
Some abilities apply their spirit scaling not in increased damage, but in other aspects of how they work.  These vary wildly, and include
Lash
's
Death Slam
which increases its throwing range, and
McGinnis
's
Medicinal Specter
which extends its duration.

guides.title: Ability Duration
guides.content:
Ability Duration
determines how long the effect of abilities and items last.
Sources of Ability Duration
[
edit
|
edit source
]

guides.title: Boon
guides.content:
Infernus at boon level 1.
Level up notification, rewarding an
Ultimate Unlock
and a
Power Increase
.
Boons
in
Deadlock
indicate the player's power level over the course of the match. Players gain boons by gathering
Souls
, which unlocks
Ability Points
and increases their
Weapon Damage
,
Melee Damage
,
Health
and
Spirit Power
. The soul count is always visible to all players under their portrait, and the boon level can be seen in the soul container icon next to the player's soul count, and over the player's portrait by pressing Tab.
The maximum Boon level is 35.
Leveling Up
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Leveling up
in
Deadlock
is based on a hero's total gathered souls and grants
Boons
,
Ability
Unlocks
and
Ability Points
. The first three ability unlocks can only be used on non-ultimate abilities. The ultimate can be unlocked at
3.6k
.
Reward breakdown
[
edit
|
edit source
]
All heroes level up at the same souls-gathered thresholds, in the following pattern:
Every level-up grants a boon.
Levels 0, 2, 4 and 6 grant an ability unlock. Every other level grants an ability point (up to 32).
The following table details the rewards given at each threshold.
Ability Points
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Ability points
are obtained as level up rewards. Each ability has 3 tiers, requiring 1, 2, and then 5 ability points to unlock. It is possible to spend ability points in any order, including saving them to spend on an ultimate ability or for the final level of an ability.
Boon Rewards
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Haze's boon profile. (Image for reference only, numbers may be outdated)
Boon Rewards
serve to increase the base attributes of the hero. Level ups always grant a Boon Reward.
The base attributes that are improved by Boons are: base
bullet damage
, base
melee damage
,
spirit power
, and base
health
. Each hero gains unique amounts of base stats from their Boons. The differences in these boon profiles can dictate which heroes are more powerful in the early stages of a game and which heroes become powerful in the late game. Some heroes may have additional stats improved by Boons, such as
Dynamo
's
bullet resist
.
Boon Tables
[
edit
|
edit source
]
See also
Hero Attributes Table
for dynamic boon stats.

guides.title: Cheater Frog
guides.content:
Cheater frog portrait
Abrams
in the "Cheater's Choice" pop-up
The
Cheater Frog
is an anti-cheat mechanic in
Deadlock
.
Overview
[
edit
|
edit source
]
When the game's anti-cheat algorithm identifies a player as a cheater, a pop-up appears to the other players to choose between banning the player immediately or turning them into a frog before banning. The frog is unable to perform any actions besides moving. The match also becomes free to leave and the result is not counted for matchmaking.
Trivia
[
edit
|
edit source
]
The Cheater Frog was based on an unused mechanic designed for
Counter Strike: Global Offensive
that turned cheaters into chickens.
[
1
]
The Cheater Frog model is the same used in
Pocket
's
Affliction
. The frog was also originally used for
Sinclair
's
Rabbit Hex
.
References
[
edit
|
edit source
]
↑
2024-09-26 -
@BurtonJ
on
X
-
"Holy crap, they finally built codename 'FU Surprise Mode'?! When I worked on VAC, Rich and I designed this for CS but the cheater was a chicken."

guides.title: Console commands
guides.content:
Console commands allow you to modify gameplay settings. If the player has the "Dev command console" option enabled in settings, pressing
F7
will open the developer console, which allows you to run commands.
Console commands can be used in the
Hideout
, Sandbox and "Explore NYC" modes, as well as cheat-enabled custom games.
All Known Convars/Commands
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Useful Console Commands
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Commands that require cheats will not work in standard multiplayer matches. To enable cheats in a custom lobby, run
sv_cheats true
.
Auto execute console commands on startup
[
edit
|
edit source
]
In the
<installation path>\steamapps\common\Deadlock\game\citadel\cfg
folder create a text file name it
autoexec.cfg
Put the console commands that you want to run on startup of Deadlock in the
autoexec.cfg
file, one console command on each line and save it
In the Steam app right-click Deadlock in your list of games on the Library tab and select Properties...
In the "LAUNCH OPTIONS" field enter
-console -exec autoexec
Hosting Custom Games
[
edit
|
edit source
]
The host starts a custom game by loading into the map with
map dl_midtown
You may want to pause the game immediately after joining by pressing
P
to give other players time to join the match.
The host finds their ID by running
status
Find a green line in the console that looks like this:
[Client] steamid : [A:1:XXXXXXXXXX:XXXXX] (XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX)
(the Xs will be replaced with numbers)
Select the part after
steamid
that looks like this, including the square brackets:
[A:1:XXXXXXXXXX:XXXXX]
Right click and choose "Copy Selected Text"
The host sends players the ID they just copied. Players can now connect to the game by opening their console and running
connect [A:1:XXXXXXXXXX:XXXXX]
(replace the part after connect with the ID they got from the host).
Upon joining, players will be able to select their team and hero. To bring up this menu again, players can run
changeteam 0
.
If you ever want to restart the game, the host can run
changelevel dl_midtown
.
Adding Bots
[
edit
|
edit source
]
On the main menu, before you start the game, run
exec citadel_botmatch_practice_6v6
to load the bot config.
If you're already in a game, you can perform these steps then run
changelevel dl_midtown
to restart the game instead of starting a new one.
Run
citadel_spawn_practice_bots false
so that bots won't spawn immediately when the game starts.
Run
citadel_spawn_practice_bots_count 12
to ensure that bots fill all remaining slots (they'll be added until both teams have 6 players).
Start the game (i.e.
map street_test
) and press
P
to pause the game during the pregame (as soon as you load in).
You can use
citadel_pregame_duration
before starting the game to increase the duration of the pregame if you want.
Allow all other human players to connect and select their team and hero.
The team/hero menu can be a bit glitchy. Players can use the
changeteam
command instead (see table above for command description).
Remember to choose your (the host's) own team and hero as well. This may automatically unpause the game - press
P
after selecting a team to pause again.
After all human players have joined teams and selected heroes, the host runs
citadel_spawn_practice_bots true
, then presses
P
again to unpause the game. Bots should fill the remaining slots on each team.

guides.title: Cosmic Veil
guides.content:
https://deadlock.wiki/File:Veil_Greyside.mp4
The Grey side of a Cosmic Veil. Heroes and their Minions are obscured on the other side.
https://deadlock.wiki/File:Veil_Rainbowside.mp4
The Rainbow side of a Cosmic Veil. Heroes and their Minions are visible on the other side.
Cosmic Veils
are visual barriers located around the
Map
at all
Juke Rooms
and beneath most structural arches. They do not block movement, but slightly obscure vision beyond the barrier acting like one-way window film. Depending on the side, the barrier will either be of a gray colorless filter or rainbow shine filter.
On the gray colorless side, it will block out any perception of color and more importantly hide all enemy heroes as well as abilities performed by any player from the opposite side. Several visual effects will be visible but most player created game objects cannot be seen through the gray side.
Meanwhile, the rainbow shine side will allow players to see others on the other side of the cosmic veil. As such, this game mechanic is often used as a means in
positioning
to attack enemies with surprise or escaping from a chase.
Notable Interactions
[
edit
|
edit source
]
An
item
that takes advantage of the abundance of these cosmic veils while providing additional benefits from their usage is the Tier 3
Vitality
Item
,
Veil Walker
, which can be purchased from the
Shop
for
3,200
.
The grey side of the veil breaks line of sight for abilities like
Billy
's
Chain Gang
,
Lash
's
Death Slam
and
McGinnis
'
Mini Turrets
. The rainbow side of the veil breaks line of sight for Death Slam but not for other abilities.
Trivia
[
edit
|
edit source
]
The rainbow shine side of a cosmic veil is based on a real world natural phenomenon called
thin-film interference
, where white light is split into different wavelengths creating a rainbow pattern on a thin-film formed on soap, oil, types of protective coating, etc.

guides.title: Crates
guides.content:
Crates
Crates (or boxes) are
breakable
objects scattered around the map that, when broken, have a chance to drop
Unsecured Souls
. Crates can be broken by shooting, punching, using certain abilities, dashing, or sliding through them.
Overview
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Crates spawn after 2 in-game minutes (with the exception of a large group of breakables placed in the room directly above the
Mid-Boss
pit, which instead spawn after 10 in-game minutes), and respawn 3 minutes after being broken. Souls provided from crates are Unsecured Souls. Not collecting the souls and leaving them on the ground will delay the respawn if they aren't collected within the normal respawn time.
Souls collected from crates are tracked in the post-game "Souls by Source" graph as "Breakable Pickups".
Soul Values
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Value:
Soul Value of the target at the very beginning of a match
Gain:
How the soul value changes over the time of the match, measured in minutes
2 min:
Value of the target at the initial moment of spawn
10 min/20 min/30 min:
Value of the target at certain game times
Notes
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Even though crates spawn after 2 in-game minutes, their soul scaling starts immediately.
Update history
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Lore
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Crates have the
Fairfax Industries
logo.
Trivia
[
edit
|
edit source
]
There are 518 crates on the map
Gallery
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Crate spawn locations

guides.title: Crowd Control
guides.content:
See also
Status Effects
for a comprehensive list of all sources of Crowd Control and other Status Effects.
Crowd Control (CC)
refers to
effects
that alter an opponent's capability to function, including their movement, ability use, weapon firing, and many others.
They can be applied both by
Hero
abilities and
Items
from the
Shop
.
Types of Crowd Control
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Movement Slow
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Reduces the target's movement speed by a percentage.
For example, a 30% slow reduces a 10 meters / second movement speed to 7 m/s.
Movement Slow cannot reduce a target's speed below 2 meters / second.
Stun
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Prevents the target from shooting, using abilities, using items, and moving. Stun also causes the target to be
Interrupted
.
Examples include
Infernus
'
Concussive Combustion
,
Ivy
's
Stone Form
, and
Dynamo
's
Singularity
.
Sleep
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Similar to
Stun
, it prevents the target from shooting and using abilities. However, unlike Stun, it
does not
prevent movement; instead, it slows the target to 2m/s.
Sleep does not
Interrupt
channels. Receiving any damage wakes the target instantly.
Sources of Sleep include abilities such as
Haze
's
Sleep Dagger
and
Rem
's
Naptime
.
Immobilize
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Reduces all speed to 0 and stops all voluntary movement, though the target can still be displaced by others. Additionally, it prevents the use of movement related abilities. It removes all upwards acceleration, making airborne targets fall to the ground.
Examples include
Warden
's
Binding Word
,
Paige
's
Captivating Read
, and
Arctic Blast
.
Silence
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Prevents the target from activating abilities, though they can still use
Item
actives.
A common cause of confusion is that
Silence
does not
Interrupt
, meaning abilities that have started will always continue through Silence, such as
Seven
's
Storm Cloud
.
Examples include
Silence Wave
,
Silencer
,
Focus Lens
,
Wraith
's
Telekinesis
, and more.
Item Silence
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Prevents the target from using
Item
actives, though they can still use abilities.
Examples include
Cursed Relic
(disables all items) and
Slowing Hex
(disables movement items).
Disarm
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Prevents the target from firing their main weapon but melee attacks can still be used. Note that abilities that use weapon-damage can still be used while
Disarmed
, such as
Haze
's
Bullet Dance
.
Examples include
Mo & Krill
's
Sand Blast
and
Disarming Hex
.
Interrupt
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Interrupts instantly cancel all ability casts and channels. It alone does not prevent further use of abilities. When targets are
Stunned
they are also Interrupted.
Examples of Interrupt (other than Stun) include the
Cursed Relic
and
Knockdown
Items
and
Grey Talon
's
Spirit Snare
.
Displace
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Exerts some force on the target that modifies their acceleration or velocity, moving or knocking them around.
Examples include
Lash
's
Ground Strike
,
Bebop
's
Exploding Uppercut
and
Grapple Arm
,
Vindicta
's
Stake
, the
Rescue Beam
and
Knockdown
Items
, and more.
Time Stop
[
edit
|
edit source
]
The target acts as if time is frozen, pausing their movement, animations, casts, channels.
The effects of time stopped abilities can still function. For example, if
Dynamo
is time stopped during his
Singularity
channel, it still pulls and stuns enemies. Note that this technically lengthens the duration of the
Singularity
.
The only source of Time Stop is
Paradox
's
Kinetic Carbine
.
Hex
[
edit
|
edit source
]
The target is transformed into a rabbit.
The target gains a smaller model and hitbox and moves faster, but will take increased damage. Hexed targets cannot use their weapon, melee, abilities, or items. Abilities and heavy melee attacks will not be interrupted when hexed.
The only source of Hex is
Sinclair
's
Rabbit Hex
.
Crowd Control Reductions and Immunities
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Effects that reduce the intensity of crowd control, either partially or completely.
Slow Resistance
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Reduce the effectiveness of slows.
𝑀
𝑜
𝑑
𝑖

For example,
Infernus
'
Flame Dash
normally moves at 12 meters per second, but with a 30% slow and its innate 50% slow resistance it is slowed down by 15% to 10.2m/s.
Invincibility
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Become unable to be hit or targeted. All projectiles and area effects phase through while you also can not be targeted by
Items
such as
Cursed Relic
or
Abilities
such as
Mo & Krill
's
Combo
.
Examples include
Ethereal Shift
and
Calico
's
Return to Shadows
and the transformation phase of
Yamato
's
Shadow Transformation
.
While
Yamato
cannot move during the transformation phase, both
Ethereal Shift
and
Return to Shadows
allow limited
movement
.
Purge
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Instantly remove all debuffs.
Some debuffs delay the crucial negative effect, such as
Mirage
's
Djinn's Mark
. Therefore, if the mark is applied, then the target purges their debuffs, the mark never detonates.
Examples include
Viscous
'
The Cube
(Tier 3),
Yamato
's
Shadow Transformation
, and
Dispel Magic
.
Unstoppable
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Become immune to all forms of crowd control.
For example the
Unstoppable
Item
,
Abrams
'
Seismic Impact
(Tier 3), and
Yamato
's
Shadow Transformation
.

guides.title: Death
guides.content:
“
Death brings consequences once more.
— The
Patrons
https://deadlock.wiki/File:Music_stinger_player_death.mp3
Music stinger for the player's death.
Death
occurs when a
hero
takes sufficient damage to be reduced to zero health. Upon dying, the player's screen is shaded gray, a death timer is displayed over their hero's icon,
souls
are rewarded to heroes who earned a kill or an assist, and other hero or situation-specific events may occur, as detailed below.
Overview
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Upon dying, heroes will drop any of their unsecured souls in a spherical container that can then be picked up by other heroes.
While dead, players can browse the
shop
, spectate their teammates, use pings and talk in chat. If all allies are dead, only the
Patron
's perspective can be seen. Clicking an objective on the
Minimap
allows the player to view the perspective of that objective.
Once the countdown reaches zero, heroes will respawn back in base.
Respawn Time
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Example of a respawn timer
https://deadlock.wiki/File:Music_stinger_player_respawn_countdown.mp3
Music stinger played before the end of respawn countdown.
https://deadlock.wiki/File:Music_stinger_player_respawn.mp3
Music stinger played when the player respawns
As the game progresses, the players will take longer to respawn. The respawn time increases linearly based on the following times:
The respawn time is capped at 85s.
Dying to a non-player
Unit
(when there is no bounty/assist from an enemy Hero) will increase the respawn time by 10s.
Comeback kill
[
edit
|
edit source
]
After 10 minutes in a match, killing a player whose net worth is above 15% higher than the killer team's average net worth increases that player's respawn time. The increase varies based on the net worth difference and match duration, from +6s for 15% net worth at 10 minutes to +22s for 30% net worth at 25 minutes.
Last Stand Buff
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Killing the first stage of a
Patron
shortens the remaining respawn time of any dead player in the defending team, as follows:
If the remaining time is 30s or above, it is shortened by 20s (e.g. 45s shortened to 25s);
If it's between 10s and 30s, it is shortened to 10s;
At 10s and below, the respawn time is unchanged.
This effect is only applied once, at the moment the Patron is killed. Subsequent deaths will have the regular respawn time.
Rejuvenator
Crystal Revive Tokens
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Revive Tokens are obtained from the Crystal that drops upon killing the
Mid-Boss
. The Crystal contains a total of 3 Revive Tokens.
Revive Tokens can only be claimed by a
Heavy Melee
, and only 1 is granted per punch.
Revive Tokens are consumed upon a players death, and any further deaths from that player will not consume more. Once all the Tokens are consumed, the entire team will lose access to the mid-boss buff.
Killing a player with a Rejuvenator credit does not appear as a kill in the HUD and does not grant any
souls
, but it does trigger "on hero kill" effects such as the cooldown reset of
Shiv
's
Killing Blow
or the
stack
buildup of
Grey Talon
's,
Mo & Krill
's and
Vindicta
's ultimates. The same happens when triggering
Victor
's
Shocking Reanimation
.
Update history
[
edit
|
edit source
]
See all updates:
Death Update history
Trivia
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Soul Rebirth
was an item that made the player respawn 4s after their death, in the location where they died. It also increased the regular respawn time, when the player died while Soul Rebirth was on cooldown. This item has been removed from the game.

guides.title: Early Development Build
guides.content:
The Early Development Build is the closed beta
[
1
]
of
Deadlock
.
It possesses a wide array of placeholder designs and models. In order to access the build, one must first receive an invite from someone who already has access.
The official
Deadlock
forums
were created on April 3, 2024, and the first posts on the forum were made on April 16.
[
2
]
The
official Discord server
was created on April 20. Matchmaking was opened for the first time on April 24.
[
3
]
Players can create an account on the forums and join the Discord server through a link in the game's main menu screen, in the lower right corner.
Invitation Process
[
edit
|
edit source
]
During the beta an invitation tree system is used to handle access to the game.
When invited, players would receive an email containing details for logging into the game's official forums as well as instructions on how to activate the game via Steam.
Initially, players with access to the game could invite others by manually sending a request to an official address or by submitting recruitment requests on the forums. The initial intention with this system was to gradually expand the player base to allow Deadlock players to play the game with their "usual friend group" so long as they were trusted and could play on U.S. servers.
In the
May 5th, 2024 Update
the game client was updated with the Recommend A Friend system which involved submitting the invitation recipient's email address, region and a short piece of information about who was being invited. Until additional server regions were added starting with EU servers in the
June 20th, 2024 Update
, only U.S. region requests were accepted though no verification was in place to prevent non-U.S. players from being invited by setting the region falsely.
The Recommend A Friend system was then reworked in the
June 6th, 2024 Update
to instead utilise Steam. The new system had a recency requirement, meaning that the inviter needed be friends with the invitee for a specific amount of time before sending an invite was allowed and limited accounts could not be invited.
In the June 20th, 2024 Update, the recency requirement was removed due to concerns about inviting friends who weren't initially added as Steam friends as well as additional systems being added allowing the developers to better filter invitations.
Embargo
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Despite various leaks, an embargo was initially in place preventing sharing of information about Deadlock by those with access. On August 23rd, 2024, Yoshi announced via the forums and official Discord server that this restriction was being lifted and allowing for sharing of information about the game including creating videos and streaming. Subsequently, the official forums were made publicly viewable.
Curfew
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Until the
September 12th, 2024 Update
matchmaking and PvP server uptime was restricted to specific periods for each server region. It was possible to circumvent this by changing server regions via a console command (which was allowed though discouraged by the developers).
Following the update, servers and matchmaking for all regions were set to be open 24/7.
Feedback Process
[
edit
|
edit source
]
During the beta, feedback is heavily encouraged. However, due to developer concerns about echo chambers of opinions being formed, it is encouraged that all feedback be given via the game's official forums. The forums contain various sections for different kinds of feedback and have permissions set so that users can only view their own posts. In theory this helps prevent players from giving feedback simply because they saw others giving the same feedback and helps the developers better zero in on points where several players have independently given the same feedback.
Players can also suggest new features, heroes and items for the game via the forums, some of which have been added, such as
Magic Carpet
.
Experimental Build
[
edit
|
edit source
]
In May 2025, a second private
Deadlock
build was accidentally shared in a livestream, revealing then-upcoming new heroes and redesigns for existing heroes.
[
4
]
References
[
edit
|
edit source
]
↑
Listed as
"Deadlock - Closed Beta Access"
under Steam user licenses
↑
2024-04-16 -
Yoshi
; Deadlock Forums -
Early Development Build Privacy
↑
2024-04-24 -
Yoshi
; Deadlock Discord -
"Reminder that matchmaking games start tonight at 8PM PST. Matches will start one after the other as the queue fills up. We are excited to start playtesting with you all."
↑
2025-05-01 - atahutahatena on Reddit.com/r/GamingLeaksAndRumors -
Streamer accidentally leaks hero roster of a more "secret" private Deadlock pre-alpha playtest build

Extra slots

item
slots that are unlocked after certain objectives are completed over the course of a match.

guides.title: Golden Statue
guides.content:
Gold statue render
Golden Statues
are small
breakable
gold objects found in various set locations around the map. When broken, they have a chance to drop a pick-up that grants a permanent stat buff.
Overview
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Golden Statues will begin to spawn after 2 minutes has elapsed in the match. When destroyed, they have a 50% chance to drop a random stat buff. All buffs obtained are permanent when picked up. The statue will respawn 3 minutes after being broken.
Golden Statues Buffs have 3 levels that depend on the time when the buff drops and increase the stat bonuses. Level 1 buffs can be obtained immediately, Level 2 buffs can be obtained after 10 minutes, and Level 3 buffs can be obtained after 30 minutes.
An additional group of 8 Golden Statues will spawn inside the room above
Mid-Boss
after 10 minutes (thus they will grant Level 2 buffs).
The current stat bonuses gained by Gold Statues can be found by mousing over the Weapon, Vitality, or Spirit stats in the bottom left and then mousing over the appropriate stat.
Sources
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Besides the statues, the following sources also grant buffs:
Golden Goose Egg
Sinner's Sacrifices
Mid-Boss
Eternal Gift
(
Street Brawl
)
Buffs received from these sources are random and follow the same level scaling as the statues.
Note:
Powerups
also grant buffs, but unlike statue buffs those are temporary, have a different scaling, and scale linearly instead of having fixed values.
Buffs Granted
[
edit
|
edit source
]
Update history
[
edit
|
edit source
]
See all updates:
Golden Statue Update history
Gallery
[
edit
|
edit source
]
In-game image
Ammo buff
Cooldown buff
Fire Rate buff
Health buff
Spirit Power buff
Weapon Damage buff
